                          byte[] value,\\n381:                                               Header[] headers,\\n382:                                               AppendCallbacks callbacks,\\n383:                                               ByteBuffer buffer,\\n384:                                               long nowMs) {\\n385:         assert partition != RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION;\\n386: \\n387:         RecordAppendResult appendResult = tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers, callbacks, dq, nowMs);\\n388:         if (appendResult != null) {\\n389:             // Somebody else found us a batch, return the one we waited for! Hopefully this doesn't happen often...\\n390:             return appendResult;\\n391:         }\\n392: \\n393:         MemoryRecordsBuilder recordsBuilder = recordsBuilder(buffer);\\n394:         ProducerBatch batch = new ProducerBatch(new TopicPartition(topic, partition), recordsBuilder, nowMs);\\n395:         FutureRecordMetadata future = Objects.requireNonNull(batch.tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers,\\n396:                 callbacks, nowMs));\\n397: \\n398:         dq.addLast(batch);\\n399:         incomplete.add(batch);\\n400: \\n401:         return new RecordAppendResult(future, dq.size() > 1 || batch.isFull(), true, batch.estimatedSizeInBytes());\\n402:     }\\n403: \\n404:     private MemoryRecordsBuilder recordsBuilder(ByteBuffer buffer) {\\n405:         return MemoryRecords.builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, 0L);\\n406:     }\\n407: \\n408:     /**\\n409:      * Check if all batches in the queue are full.\\n410:      */\\n411:     private boolean allBatchesFull(Deque<ProducerBatch> deque) {\\n412:         // Only the last batch may be incomplete, so we just check that.\\n413:         ProducerBatch last = deque.peekLast();\\n414:         return last == null || last.isFull();\\n415:     }\\n416: \\n417:      /**\\n418:      *  Try to append to a ProducerBatch.\\n419:      *\\n420:      *  If it is full, we return null and a new batch is created. We also close the batch for record appends to free up\\n421:      *  resources like compression buffers. The batch will be fully closed (ie. the record batch headers will be written\\n422:      *  and memory records built) in one of the following cases (whichever comes first): right before send,\\n423:      *  if it is expired, or when the producer is closed.\\n424:      */\\n425:     private RecordAppendResult tryAppend(long timestamp, byte[] key, byte[] value, Header[] headers,\\n426:                                          Callback callback, Deque<ProducerBatch> deque, long nowMs) {\\n427:         if (closed)\\n428:             throw new KafkaException(\\\"Producer closed while send in progress\\\");\\n429:         ProducerBatch last = deque.peekLast();\\n430:         if (last != null) {\\n431:             int initialBytes = last.estimatedSizeInBytes();\\n432:             FutureRecordMetadata future = last.tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers, callback, nowMs);\\n433:             if (future == null) {\\n434:                 last.closeForRecordAppends();\\n435:             } else {\\n436:                 int appendedBytes = last.estimatedSizeInBytes() - initialBytes;\\n437:                 return new RecordAppendResult(future, deque.size() > 1 || last.isFull(), false, appendedBytes);\\n438:             }\\n439:         }\\n440:         return null;\\n441:     }\\n442: \\n443:     private boolean isMuted(TopicPartition tp) {\\n444:         return muted.contains(tp);\\n445:     }\\n446: \\n447:     public void resetNextBatchExpiryTime() {\\n448:         nextBatchExpiryTimeMs = Long.MAX_VALUE;\\n449:     }\\n450: \\n451:     public void maybeUpdateNextBatchExpiryTime(ProducerBatch batch) {\\n452:         if (batch.createdMs + deliveryTimeoutMs  > 0) {\\n453:             // the non-negative check is to guard us against potential overflow due to setting\\n454:             // a large value for deliveryTimeoutMs\\n455:             nextBatchExpiryTimeMs = Math.min(nextBatchExpiryTimeMs, batch.createdMs + deliveryTimeoutMs);\\n456:         } else {\\n457:             log.warn(\\\"Skipping next batch expiry time update due to addition overflow: \\\"\\n458:                 + \\\"batch.createMs={}, deliveryTimeoutMs={}\\\", batch.createdMs, deliveryTimeoutMs);\\n459:         }\\n460:     }\\n461: \\n462:     /**\\n463:      * Get a list of batches which have been sitting in the accumulator too long and need to be expired.\\n464:      */\\n465:     public List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches(long now) {\\n466:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches = new ArrayList<>();\\n467:         for (TopicInfo topicInfo : topicInfoMap.values()) {\\n468:             for (Deque<ProducerBatch> deque : topicInfo.batches.values()) {\\n469:                 // expire the batches in the order of sending\\n470:                 synchronized (deque) {\\n471:                     while (!deque.isEmpty()) {\\n472:                         ProducerBatch batch = deque.getFirst();\\n473:                         if (batch.hasReachedDeliveryTimeout(deliveryTimeoutMs, now)) {\\n474:                             deque.poll();\\n475:                             batch.abortRecordAppends();\\n476:                             expiredBatches.add(batch);\\n477:                         } else {\\n478:                             maybeUpdateNextBatchExpiryTime(batch);\\n479:                             break;\\n480:                         }\\n481:                     }\\n482:                 }\\n483:             }\\n484:         }\\n485:         return expiredBatches;\\n486:     }\\n487: \\n488:     public long getDeliveryTimeoutMs() {\\n489:         return deliveryTimeoutMs;\\n490:     }\\n491: \\n492:     /**\\n493:      * Re-enqueue the given record batch in the accumulator. In Sender.completeBatch method, we check\\n494:      * whether the batch has reached deliveryTimeoutMs or not. Hence we do not do the delivery timeout check here.\\n495:      */\\n496:     public void reenqueue(ProducerBatch batch, long now) {\\n497:         batch.reenqueued(now);\\n498:         Deque<ProducerBatch> deque = getOrCreateDeque(batch.topicPartition);\\n499:         synchronized (deque) {\\n500:             if (transactionManager != null)\\n501:                 insertInSequenceOrder(deque, batch);\\n502:             else\\n503:                 deque.addFirst(batch);\\n504:         }\\n505:     }\\n506: \\n507:     /**\\n508:      * Split the big batch that has been rejected and reenqueue the split batches in to the accumulator.\\n509:      * @return the number of split batches.\\n510:      */\\n511:     public int splitAndReenqueue(ProducerBatch bigBatch) {\\n512:         // Reset the estimated compression ratio to the initial value or the big batch compression ratio, whichever\\n513:         // is bigger. There are several different ways to do the reset. We chose the most conservative one to ensure\\n514:         // the split doesn't happen too often.\\n515:         CompressionRatioEstimator.setEstimation(bigBatch.topicPartition.topic(), compression.type(),\\n516:                                                 Math.max(1.0f, (float) bigBatch.compressionRatio()));\\n517:         Deque<ProducerBatch> dq = bigBatch.split(this.batchSize);\\n518:         int numSplitBatches = dq.size();\\n519:         Deque<ProducerBatch> partitionDequeue = getOrCreateDeque(bigBatch.topicPartition);\\n520:         while (!dq.isEmpty()) {\\n521:             ProducerBatch batch = dq.pollLast();\\n522:             incomplete.add(batch);\\n523:             // We treat the newly split batches as if they are not even tried.\\n524:             synchronized (partitionDequeue) {\\n525:                 if (transactionManager != null) {\\n526:                     // We should track the newly created batches since they already have assigned sequences.\\n527:                     transactionManager.addInFlightBatch(batch);\\n528:                     insertInSequenceOrder(partitionDequeue, batch);\\n529:                 } else {\\n530:                     partitionDequeue.addFirst(batch);\\n531:                 }\\n532:             }\\n533:         }\\n534:         return numSplitBatches;\\n535:     }\\n536: \\n537:     // We will have to do extra work to ensure the queue is in order when requests are being retried and there are\\n538:     // multiple requests in flight to that partition. If the first in flight request fails to append, then all the\\n539:     // subsequent in flight requests will also fail because the sequence numbers will not be accepted.\\n540:     //\\n541:     // Further, once batches are being retried, we are reduced to a single in flight request for that partition. So when\\n542:     // the subsequent batches come back in sequence order, they will have to be placed further back in the queue.\\n543:     //\\n544:     // Note that this assumes that all the batches in the queue which have an assigned sequence also have the current\\n545:     // producer id. We will not attempt to reorder messages if the producer id has changed, we will throw an\\n546:     // IllegalStateException instead.\\n547:     private void insertInSequenceOrder(Deque<ProducerBatch> deque, ProducerBatch batch) {\\n548:         // When we are re-enqueueing and have enabled idempotence, the re-enqueued batch must always have a sequence.\\n549:         if (batch.baseSequence() == RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE)\\n550:             throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Trying to re-enqueue a batch which doesn't have a sequence even \\\" +\\n551:                 \\\"though idempotency is enabled.\\\");\\n552: \\n553:         if (!transactionManager.hasInflightBatches(batch.topicPartition))\\n554:             throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"We are re-enqueueing a batch which is not tracked as part of the in flight \\\" +\\n555:                 \\\"requests. batch.topicPartition: \\\" + batch.topicPartition + \\\"; batch.baseSequence: \\\" + batch.baseSequence());\\n556: \\n557:         ProducerBatch firstBatchInQueue = deque.peekFirst();\\n558:         if (firstBatchInQueue != null && firstBatchInQueue.hasSequence() && firstBatchInQueue.baseSequence() < batch.baseSequence()) {\\n559:             // The incoming batch can't be inserted at the front of the queue without violating the sequence ordering.\\n560:             // This means that the incoming batch should be placed somewhere further back.\\n561:             // We need to find the right place for the incoming batch and insert it there.\\n562:             // We will only enter this branch if we have multiple inflights sent to different brokers and we need to retry\\n563:             // the inflight batches.\\n564:             //\\n565:             // Since we reenqueue exactly one batch a time and ensure that the queue is ordered by sequence always, it\\n566:             // is a simple linear scan of a subset of the in flight batches to find the right place in the queue each time.\\n567:             List<ProducerBatch> orderedBatches = new ArrayList<>();\\n568:             while (deque.peekFirst() != null && deque.peekFirst().hasSequence() && deque.peekFirst().baseSequence() < batch.baseSequence())\\n569:                 orderedBatches.add(deque.pollFirst());\\n570: \\n571:             log.debug(\\\"Reordered incoming batch with sequence {} for partition {}. It was placed in the queue at \\\" +\\n572:                 \\\"position {}\\\", batch.baseSequence(), batch.topicPartition, orderedBatches.size());\\n573:             // Either we have reached a point where there are batches without a sequence (ie. never been drained\\n574:             // and are hence in order by default), or the batch at the front of the queue has a sequence greater\\n575:             // than the incoming batch. This is the right place to add the incoming batch.\\n576:             deque.addFirst(batch);\\n577: \\n578:             // Now we have to re insert the previously queued batches in the right order.\\n579:             for (int i = orderedBatches.size() - 1; i >= 0; --i) {\\n580:                 deque.addFirst(orderedBatches.get(i));\\n581:             }\\n582: \\n583:             // At this point, the incoming batch has been queued in the correct place according to its sequence.\\n584:         } else {\\n585:             deque.addFirst(batch);\\n586:         }\\n587:     }\\n588: \\n589:     /**\\n590:      * Add the leader to the ready nodes if the batch is ready\\n591:      *\\n592:      * @param exhausted 'true' is the buffer pool is exhausted\\n593:      * @param part The partition\\n594:      * @param leader The leader for the partition\\n595:      * @param waitedTimeMs How long batch waited\\n596:      * @param backingOff Is backing off\\n597:      * @param backoffAttempts Number of attempts for calculating backoff delay\\n598:      * @param full Is batch full\\n599:      * @param nextReadyCheckDelayMs The delay for next check\\n600:      * @param readyNodes The set of ready nodes (to be filled in)\\n601:      * @return The delay for next check\\n602:      */\\n603:     private long batchReady(boolean exhausted, TopicPartition part, Node leader,\\n604:                             long waitedTimeMs, boolean backingOff, int backoffAttempts,\\n605:                             boolean full, long nextReadyCheckDelayMs, Set<Node> readyNodes) {\\n606:         if (!readyNodes.contains(leader) && !isMuted(part)) {\\n607:             long timeToWaitMs = backingOff ? retryBackoff.backoff(backoffAttempts > 0 ? backoffAttempts - 1 : 0) : lingerMs;\\n608:             boolean expired = waitedTimeMs >= timeToWaitMs;\\n609:             boolean transactionCompleting = transactionManager != null && transactionManager.isCompleting();\\n610:             boolean sendable = full\\n611:                     || expired\\n612:                     || exhausted\\n613:                     || closed\\n614:                     || flushInProgress()\\n615:                     || transactionCompleting;\\n616:             if (sendable && !backingOff) {\\n617:                 readyNodes.add(leader);\\n618:             } else {\\n619:                 long timeLeftMs = Math.max(timeToWaitMs - waitedTimeMs, 0);\\n620:                 // Note that this results in a conservative estimate since an un-sendable partition may have\\n621:                 // a leader that will later be found to have sendable data. However, this is good enough\\n622:                 // since we'll just wake up and then sleep again for the remaining time.\\n623:                 nextReadyCheckDelayMs = Math.min(timeLeftMs, nextReadyCheckDelayMs);\\n624:             }\\n625:         }\\n626:         return nextReadyCheckDelayMs;\\n627:     }\\n628: \\n629:     /**\\n630:      * Iterate over partitions to see which one have batches ready and collect leaders of those\\n631:      * partitions into the set of ready nodes.  If partition has no leader, add the topic to the set\\n632:      * of topics with no leader.  This function also calculates stats for adaptive partitioning.\\n633:      *\\n634:      * @param metadataSnapshot      The cluster metadata\\n635:      * @param nowMs                 The current time\\n636:      * @param topic                 The topic\\n637:      * @param topicInfo             The topic info\\n638:      * @param nextReadyCheckDelayMs The delay for next check\\n639:      * @param readyNodes            The set of ready nodes (to be filled in)\\n640:      * @param unknownLeaderTopics   The set of topics with no leader (to be filled in)\\n641:      * @return The delay for next check\\n642:      */\\n643:     private long partitionReady(MetadataSnapshot metadataSnapshot, long nowMs, String topic,\\n644:                                 TopicInfo topicInfo,\\n645:                                 long nextReadyCheckDelayMs, Set<Node> readyNodes, Set<String> unknownLeaderTopics) {\\n646:         ConcurrentMap<Integer, Deque<ProducerBatch>> batches = topicInfo.batches;\\n647:         // Collect the queue sizes for available partitions to be used in adaptive partitioning.\\n648:         int[] queueSizes = null;\\n649:         int[] partitionIds = null;\\n650:         if (enableAdaptivePartitioning && batches.size() >= metadataSnapshot.cluster().partitionsForTopic(topic).size()) {\\n651:             // We don't do adaptive partitioning until we scheduled at least a batch for all\\n652:             // partitions (i.e. we have the corresponding entries in the batches map), we just\\n653:             // do uniform.  The reason is that we build queue sizes from the batches map,\\n654:             // and if an entry is missing in the batches map, then adaptive partitioning logic\\n655:             // won't know about it and won't switch to it.\\n656:             queueSizes = new int[batches.size()];\\n657:             partitionIds = new int[queueSizes.length];\\n658:         }\\n659: \\n660:         int queueSizesIndex = -1;\\n661:         boolean exhausted = this.free.queued() > 0;\\n662:         for (Map.Entry<Integer, Deque<ProducerBatch>> entry : batches.entrySet()) {\\n663:             TopicPartition part = new TopicPartition(topic, entry.getKey());\\n664:             // Advance queueSizesIndex so that we properly index available\\n665:             // partitions.  Do it here so that it's done for all code paths.\\n666: \\n667:             Node leader = metadataSnapshot.cluster().leaderFor(part);\\n668:             if (leader != null && queueSizes != null) {\\n669:                 ++queueSizesIndex;\\n670:                 assert queueSizesIndex < queueSizes.length;\\n671:                 partitionIds[queueSizesIndex] = part.partition();\\n672:             }\\n673: \\n674:             Deque<ProducerBatch> deque = entry.getValue();\\n675: \\n676:             final long waitedTimeMs;\\n677:             final boolean backingOff;\\n678:             final int backoffAttempts;\\n679:             final int dequeSize;\\n680:             final boolean full;\\n681: \\n682:             OptionalInt leaderEpoch = metadataSnapshot.leaderEpochFor(part);\\n683: \\n684:             // This loop is especially hot with large partition counts. So -\\n685: \\n686:             // 1. We should avoid code that increases synchronization between application thread calling\\n687:             // send(), and background thread running runOnce(), see https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/KAFKA-16226\\n688: \\n689:             // 2. We are careful to only perform the minimum required inside the\\n690:             // synchronized block, as this lock is also used to synchronize producer threads\\n691:             // attempting to append() to a partition/batch.\\n692: \\n693:             synchronized (deque) {\\n694:                 // Deques are often empty in this path, esp with large partition counts,\\n695:                 // so we exit early if we can.\\n696:                 ProducerBatch batch = deque.peekFirst();\\n697:                 if (batch == null) {\\n698:                     continue;\\n699:                 }\\n700: \\n701:                 waitedTimeMs = batch.waitedTimeMs(nowMs);\\n702:                 batch.maybeUpdateLeaderEpoch(leaderEpoch);\\n703:                 backingOff = shouldBackoff(batch.hasLeaderChangedForTheOngoingRetry(), batch, waitedTimeMs);\\n704:                 backoffAttempts = batch.attempts();\\n705:                 dequeSize = deque.size();\\n706:                 full = dequeSize > 1 || batch.isFull();\\n707:             }\\n708: \\n709:             if (leader == null) {\\n710:                 // This is a partition for which leader is not known, but messages are available to send.\\n711:                 // Note that entries are currently not removed from batches when deque is empty.\\n712:                 unknownLeaderTopics.add(part.topic());\\n713:             } else {\\n714:                 if (queueSizes != null)\\n715:                     queueSizes[queueSizesIndex] = dequeSize;\\n716:                 if (partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs > 0) {\\n717:                     // Check if we want to exclude the partition from the list of available partitions\\n718:                     // if the broker hasn't responded for some time.\\n719:                     NodeLatencyStats nodeLatencyStats = nodeStats.get(leader.id());\\n720:                     if (nodeLatencyStats != null) {\\n721:                         // NOTE: there is no synchronization between reading metrics,\\n722:                         // so we read ready time first to avoid accidentally marking partition\\n723:                         // unavailable if we read while the metrics are being updated.\\n724:                         long readyTimeMs = nodeLatencyStats.readyTimeMs;\\n725:                         if (readyTimeMs - nodeLatencyStats.drainTimeMs > partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs)\\n726:                             --queueSizesIndex;\\n727:                     }\\n728:                 }\\n729: \\n730:                 nextReadyCheckDelayMs = batchReady(exhausted, part, leader, waitedTimeMs, backingOff,\\n731:                     backoffAttempts, full, nextReadyCheckDelayMs, readyNodes);\\n732:             }\\n733:         }\\n734: \\n735:         // We've collected the queue sizes for partitions of this topic, now we can calculate\\n736:         // load stats.  NOTE: the stats are calculated in place, modifying the\\n737:         // queueSizes array.\\n738:         topicInfo.builtInPartitioner.updatePartitionLoadStats(queueSizes, partitionIds, queueSizesIndex + 1);\\n739:         return nextReadyCheckDelayMs;\\n740:     }\\n741: \\n742:     /**\\n743:      * Get a list of nodes whose partitions are ready to be sent, and the earliest time at which any non-sendable\\n744:      * partition will be ready; Also return the flag for whether there are any unknown leaders for the accumulated\\n745:      * partition batches.\\n746:      * <p>\\n747:      * A destination node is ready to send data if:\\n748:      * <ol>\\n749:      * <li>There is at least one partition that is not backing off its send\\n750:      * <li><b>and</b> those partitions are not muted (to prevent reordering if\\n751:      *   {@value org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.ProducerConfig#MAX_IN_FLIGHT_REQUESTS_PER_CONNECTION}\\n752:      *   is set to one)</li>\\n753:      * <li><b>and <i>any</i></b> of the following are true</li>\\n754:      * <ul>\\n755:      *     <li>The record set is full</li>\\n756:      *     <li>The record set has sat in the accumulator for at least lingerMs milliseconds</li>\\n757:      *     <li>The accumulator is out of memory and threads are blocking waiting for data (in this case all partitions\\n758:      *     are immediately considered ready).</li>\\n759:      *     <li>The accumulator has been closed</li>\\n760:      * </ul>\\n761:      * </ol>\\n762:      */\\n763:     public ReadyCheckResult ready(MetadataSnapshot metadataSnapshot, long nowMs) {\\n764:         Set<Node> readyNodes = new HashSet<>();\\n765:         long nextReadyCheckDelayMs = Long.MAX_VALUE;\\n766:         Set<String> unknownLeaderTopics = new HashSet<>();\\n767:         // Go topic by topic so that we can get queue sizes for partitions in a topic and calculate\\n768:         // cumulative frequency table (used in partitioner).\\n769:         for (Map.Entry<String, TopicInfo> topicInfoEntry : this.topicInfoMap.entrySet()) {\\n770:             final String topic = topicInfoEntry.getKey();\\n771:             nextReadyCheckDelayMs = partitionReady(metadataSnapshot, nowMs, topic, topicInfoEntry.getValue(), nextReadyCheckDelayMs, readyNodes, unknownLeaderTopics);\\n772:         }\\n773:         return new ReadyCheckResult(readyNodes, nextReadyCheckDelayMs, unknownLeaderTopics);\\n774:     }\\n775: \\n776:     /**\\n777:      * Check whether there are any batches which haven't been drained\\n778:      */\\n779:     public boolean hasUndrained() {\\n780:         for (TopicInfo topicInfo : topicInfoMap.values()) {\\n781:             for (Deque<ProducerBatch> deque : topicInfo.batches.values()) {\\n782:                 synchronized (deque) {\\n783:                     if (!deque.isEmpty())\\n784:                         return true;\\n785:                 }\\n786:             }\\n787:         }\\n788:         return false;\\n789:     }\\n790: \\n791:     private boolean shouldBackoff(boolean hasLeaderChanged, final ProducerBatch batch, final long waitedTimeMs) {\\n792:         boolean shouldWaitMore = batch.attempts() > 0 && waitedTimeMs < retryBackoff.backoff(batch.attempts() - 1);\\n793:         boolean shouldBackoff = !hasLeaderChanged && shouldWaitMore;\\n794:         if (log.isTraceEnabled()) {\\n795:             if (shouldBackoff) {\\n796:                 log.trace(\\n797:                     \\\"For {}, will backoff\\\", batch);\\n798:             } else {\\n799:                 log.trace(\\n800:                     \\\"For {}, will not backoff, shouldWaitMore {}, hasLeaderChanged {}\\\", batch,\\n801:                     shouldWaitMore, hasLeaderChanged);\\n802:             }\\n803:         } else if (log.isDebugEnabled() && hasLeaderChanged) {\\n804:             // Add less-verbose log at DEBUG.\\n805:             log.debug(\\\"For {}, leader has changed, hence skipping backoff.\\\", batch);\\n806:         }\\n807:         return shouldBackoff;\\n808:     }\\n809: \\n810:     private boolean shouldStopDrainBatchesForPartition(ProducerBatch first, TopicPartition tp) {\\n811:         ProducerIdAndEpoch producerIdAndEpoch;\\n812:         if (transactionManager != null) {\\n813:             if (!transactionManager.isSendToPartitionAllowed(tp))\\n814:                 return true;\\n815: \\n816:             producerIdAndEpoch = transactionManager.producerIdAndEpoch();\\n817:             if (!producerIdAndEpoch.isValid())\\n818:                 // we cannot send the batch until we have refreshed the producer id\\n819:                 return true;\\n820: \\n821:             if (!first.hasSequence()) {\\n822:                 if (transactionManager.hasInflightBatches(tp) && transactionManager.hasStaleProducerIdAndEpoch(tp)) {\\n823:                     // Don't drain any new batches while the partition has in-flight batches with a different epoch\\n824:                     // and/or producer ID. Otherwise, a batch with a new epoch and sequence number\\n825:                     // 0 could be written before earlier batches complete, which would cause out of sequence errors\\n826:                     return true;\\n827:                 }\\n828: \\n829:                 if (transactionManager.hasUnresolvedSequence(first.topicPartition))\\n830:                     // Don't drain any new batches while the state of previous sequence numbers\\n831:                     // is unknown. The previous batches would be unknown if they were aborted\\n832:                     // on the client after being sent to the broker at least once.\\n833:                     return true;\\n834:             }\\n835: \\n836:             int firstInFlightSequence = transactionManager.firstInFlightSequence(first.topicPartition);\\n837:             // If the queued batch already has an assigned sequence, then it is being retried.\\n838:             // In this case, we wait until the next immediate batch is ready and drain that.\\n839:             // We only move on when the next in line batch is complete (either successfully or due to\\n840:             // a fatal broker error). This effectively reduces our in flight request count to 1.\\n841:             return firstInFlightSequence != RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE && first.hasSequence()\\n842:                     && first.baseSequence() != firstInFlightSequence;\\n843:         }\\n844:         return false;\\n845:     }\\n846: \\n847:     private List<ProducerBatch> drainBatchesForOneNode(MetadataSnapshot metadataSnapshot, Node node, int maxSize, long now) {\\n848:         int size = 0;\\n849:         List<PartitionInfo> parts = metadataSnapshot.cluster().partitionsForNode(node.id());\\n850:         List<ProducerBatch> ready = new ArrayList<>();\\n851:         if (parts.isEmpty())\\n852:             return ready;\\n853:         /* to make starvation less likely each node has it's own drainIndex */\\n854:         int drainIndex = getDrainIndex(node.idString());\\n855:         int start = drainIndex = drainIndex % parts.size();\\n856:         do {\\n857:             PartitionInfo part = parts.get(drainIndex);\\n858: \\n859:             TopicPartition tp = new TopicPartition(part.topic(), part.partition());\\n860:             updateDrainIndex(node.idString(), drainIndex);\\n861:             drainIndex = (drainIndex + 1) % parts.size();\\n862:             // Only proceed if the partition has no in-flight batches.\\n863:             if (isMuted(tp))\\n864:                 continue;\\n865:             Deque<ProducerBatch> deque = getDeque(tp);\\n866:             if (deque == null)\\n867:                 continue;\\n868: \\n869:             OptionalInt leaderEpoch = metadataSnapshot.leaderEpochFor(tp);\\n870: \\n871:             final ProducerBatch batch;\\n872:             synchronized (deque) {\\n873:                 // invariant: !isMuted(tp,now) && deque != null\\n874:                 ProducerBatch first = deque.peekFirst();\\n875:                 if (first == null)\\n876:                     continue;\\n877: \\n878:                 // first != null\\n879:                 // Only drain the batch if it is not during backoff period.\\n880:                 first.maybeUpdateLeaderEpoch(leaderEpoch);\\n881:                 if (shouldBackoff(first.hasLeaderChangedForTheOngoingRetry(), first, first.waitedTimeMs(now)))\\n882:                     continue;\\n883: \\n884:                 if (size + first.estimatedSizeInBytes() > maxSize && !ready.isEmpty()) {\\n885:                     // there is a rare case that a single batch size is larger than the request size due to\\n886:                     // compression; in this case we will still eventually send this batch in a single request\\n887:                     break;\\n888:                 } else {\\n889:                     if (shouldStopDrainBatchesForPartition(first, tp))\\n890:                         break;\\n891:                 }\\n892: \\n893:                 batch = deque.pollFirst();\\n894: \\n895:                 boolean isTransactional = transactionManager != null && transactionManager.isTransactional();\\n896:                 ProducerIdAndEpoch producerIdAndEpoch =\\n897:                     transactionManager != null ? transactionManager.producerIdAndEpoch() : null;\\n898:                 if (producerIdAndEpoch != null && !batch.hasSequence()) {\\n899:                     // If the producer id/epoch of the partition do not match the latest one\\n900:                     // of the producer, we update it and reset the sequence. also be accepted, causing a duplicate.\\n909:                     //\\n910:                     // Additionally, we update the next sequence number bound for the partition, and also have\\n911:                     // the transaction manager track the batch so as to ensure that sequence ordering is maintained\\n912:                     // even if we receive out of order responses.\\n913: tp);\\n918: \\n919:                     transactionManager.addInFlightBatch(batch);\\n920:                 }\\n921:             }\\n922: \\n923:             // the rest of the work by processing outside the lock\\n924:             // close() is particularly expensive\\n925:             batch.close();\\n926:             size += batch.records().sizeInBytes();\\n927:          log.trace(\\\"Allocating a new {} byte message buffer for topic {} partition {} with remaining timeout {}ms\\\", size, topic, effectivePartition, maxTimeToBlock);\\n332:                     // This call may block if we exhausted buffer space.\\n333:                     buffer = free.allocate(size, maxTimeToBlock);\\n334:                     // Update the current time in case the buffer We also close the batch for record appends to free up\\n421:      *  resources like compression buffers. The batch will be fully closed (ie. the record batch headers will be written\\n422:      *  and memory records built) in one of the following cases (whichever comes first): right before send,\\n423:      *  if it is expired, or when the producer is closed.\\n424:      */\\n425:     private RecordAppendResult tryAppend(long timestamp, byte[] key, byte[] value, Header[] headers,\\n426:                                          Callback callback, Deque<ProducerBatch> deque, long nowMs) {\\n427:         if (closed)\\n428:             throw new KafkaException(\\\"Producer closed while send in progress\\\");\\n429:         ProducerBatch last = deque.peekLast();\\n430:         if (last != null) {\\n431:             int initialBytes = last.estimatedSizeInBytes();\\n432:             FutureRecordMetadata future = last.tryAppend(timestamp, key, value, headers, callback, nowMs);\\n433:             if (future == null) {\\n434:                 last.closeForRecordAppends();\\n435:             } else {\\n436:                 int appendedBytes = last.estimatedSizeInBytes() - initialBytes;\\n437:                 return new RecordAppendResult(future, deque.size() > 1 || last.isFull(), false, appendedBytes);\\n438:             }\\n439:         }\\n440:         return null;\\n441:     }\\n442: \\n443:     private boolean isMuted(TopicPartition tp) {\\n444:         return maybeUpdateNextBatchExpiryTime(ProducerBatch batch) {\\n452:         if (batch.createdMs + deliveryTimeoutMs  > 0) {\\n453:             // the non-negative check is to guard us against potential overflow due to setting\\n454:             // a large value for deliveryTimeoutMs\\n455:             nextBatchExpiryTimeMs = Math.min(nextBatchExpiryTimeMs, batch.createdMs + deliveryTimeoutMs);\\n456:         } else {\\n457:             log.warn(\\\"Skipping next batch expiry time update due to addition overflow: \\\"\\n458:                 + \\\"batch.createMs={}, long and need to be expired.\\n464:      */\\n465:     public List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches(long now) {\\n466:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches = new ArrayList<>();\\n467:         for (TopicInfo topicInfo : topicInfoMap.values()) {\\n468:             for (Deque<ProducerBatch> deque : topicInfo.batches.values()) {\\n469:                 // expire the batches in the order of                   expiredBatches.add(batch);\\n477:                         } else {\\n478:                             maybeUpdateNextBatchExpiryTime(batch);\\n479: In Sender.completeBatch method, we check\\n494:      * whether the batch has reached deliveryTimeoutMs or not. Hence we do not do the delivery timeout check here.\\n495:      */\\n496:     public void reenqueue(ProducerBatch batch, long now) {\\n497:         batch.reenqueued(now);\\n498:         Deque<ProducerBatch> deque = getOrCreateDeque(batch.topicPartition);\\n499:         synchronized (deque) {\\n500:             if (transactionManager != null)\\n501:                 insertInSequenceOrder(deque, batch);\\n502:             else\\n503:                 deque.addFirst(batch);\\n504:         }\\n505:     }\\n506: \\n507:     /**\\n508:      * Split the big batch that has been rejected and reenqueue the split batches in to the accumulator.\\n509:      * @return the number of split batches.\\n510:      */\\n511:     public int splitAndReenqueue(ProducerBatch bigBatch) {\\n512:         // Reset the estimated compression ratio to the initial value or the big batch compression ratio, whichever\\n513:         // is bigger. There are several different ways to do the reset. We chose the most conservative one to ensure\\n514:         // the split doesn't happen too often.\\n515:         CompressionRatioEstimator.setEstimation(bigBatch.topicPartition.topic(), compression.type(),\\n516:                                                 Math.max(1.0f, (float) bigBatch.compressionRatio()));\\n517:         Deque<ProducerBatch> dq = bigBatch.split(this.batchSize);\\n518:         int numSplitBatches = dq.size();\\n519:         Deque<ProducerBatch> partitionDequeue = getOrCreateDeque(bigBatch.topicPartition);\\n520:         while (!dq.isEmpty()) {\\n521:             ProducerBatch batch = dq.pollLast();\\n522:             incomplete.add(batch);\\n523:             // We treat the newly split batches as if they are not even tried.\\n524:             synchronized (partitionDequeue) {\\n525:                 if (transactionManager != null) {\\n526:                     // We should track the newly created batches since they already have assigned sequences.\\n527:                     transactionManager.addInFlightBatch(batch);\\n528:                     insertInSequenceOrder(partitionDequeue, batch);\\n529:                 } else {\\n530:                     partitionDequeue.addFirst(batch);\\n531:                 }\\n532:             }\\n533:         }\\n534:         return numSplitBatches;\\n535:     }\\n536: \\n537:     // We will have to do extra work to ensure the queue is in order when requests are being retried and there are\\n538:     // multiple requests in flight to that partition. If the first in flight request fails to append, then all the\\n539:     // subsequent in flight requests will also fail because the sequence numbers will not be accepted.\\n540:     //\\n541:     // Further,","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false,"persistedOutputPath":"/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/01bdda9f-9010-4ad4-89b9-60148a90d052/tool-results/buq6knk2p.txt","persistedOutputSize":69546}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016Csj8AUKFe5a5yBEehLntt","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: package org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals;\\n18: \\n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.Cluster;\\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.PartitionInfo;\\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\\n22: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\\n23: \\n24: import org.slf4j.Logger;\\n25: \\n26: import java.util.Arrays;\\n27: import java.util.List;\\n28: import java.util.concurrent.ThreadLocalRandom;\\n29: import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicInteger;\\n30: import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference;\\n31: \\n32: /**\\n33:  * Built-in default partitioner.  Note, that this is just a utility class that is used directly from\\n34:  * RecordAccumulator, it does not implement the Partitioner interface.\\n35:  *\\n36:  * The class keeps track of various bookkeeping information required for adaptive sticky partitioning\\n37:  * (described in detail in KIP-794).  There is one partitioner object per topic.\\n38:  */\\n39: public class BuiltInPartitioner {\\n40:     private final Logger log;\\n41:     private final String topic;\\n42:     private final int stickyBatchSize;\\n43: \\n44:     private volatile PartitionLoadStats partitionLoadStats = null;\\n45:     private final AtomicReference<StickyPartitionInfo> stickyPartitionInfo = new AtomicReference<>();\\n46: \\n47: \\n48:     /**\\n49:      * BuiltInPartitioner constructor.\\n50:      *\\n51:      * @param topic The topic\\n52:      * @param stickyBatchSize How much to produce to partition before switch\\n53:      */\\n54:     public BuiltInPartitioner(LogContext logContext, String topic, int stickyBatchSize) {\\n55:         this.log = logContext.logger(BuiltInPartitioner.class);\\n56:         this.topic = topic;\\n57:         if (stickyBatchSize < 1) {\\n58:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"stickyBatchSize must be >= 1 but got \\\" + stickyBatchSize);\\n59:         }\\n60:         this.stickyBatchSize = stickyBatchSize;\\n61:     }\\n62: \\n63:     /**\\n64:      * Calculate the next partition for the topic based on the partition load stats.\\n65:      */\\n66:     private int nextPartition(Cluster cluster) {\\n67:         int random = randomPartition();\\n68: \\n69:         // Cache volatile variable in local variable.\\n70:         PartitionLoadStats partitionLoadStats = this.partitionLoadStats;\\n71:         int partition;\\n72: \\n73:         if (partitionLoadStats == null) {\\n74:             // We don't have stats to do adaptive partitioning (or it's disabled), just switch to the next\\n75:             // partition based on uniform distribution.\\n76:             List<PartitionInfo> availablePartitions = cluster.availablePartitionsForTopic(topic);\\n77:             if (!availablePartitions.isEmpty()) {\\n78:                 partition = availablePartitions.get(random % availablePartitions.size()).partition();\\n79:             } else {\\n80:                 // We don't have available partitions, just pick one among all partitions.\\n81:                 List<PartitionInfo> partitions = cluster.partitionsForTopic(topic);\\n82:                 partition = random % partitions.size();\\n83:             }\\n84:         } else {\\n85:             // Calculate next partition based on load distribution.\\n86:             // Note that partitions without leader are excluded from the partitionLoadStats.\\n87:             assert partitionLoadStats.length > 0;\\n88: \\n89:             int[] cumulativeFrequencyTable = partitionLoadStats.cumulativeFrequencyTable;\\n90:             int weightedRandom = random % cumulativeFrequencyTable[partitionLoadStats.length - 1];\\n91: \\n92:             // By construction, the cumulative frequency table is sorted, so we can use binary\\n93:             // search to find the desired index.\\n94:             int searchResult = Arrays.binarySearch(cumulativeFrequencyTable, 0, partitionLoadStats.length, weightedRandom);\\n95: \\n96:             // binarySearch results the index of the found element, or -(insertion_point) - 1\\n97:             // (where insertion_point is the index of the first element greater than the key).\\n98:             // We need to get the index of the first value that is strictly greater, which\\n99:             // would be the insertion point, except if we found the element that's equal to\\n100:             // the searched value (in this case we need to get next).  For example, if we have\\n101:             //  4 5 8\\n102:             // and we're looking for 3, then we'd get the insertion_point = 0, and the function\\n103:             // would return -0 - 1 = -1, by adding 1 we'd get 0.  If we're looking for 4, we'd\\n104:             // get 0, and we need the next one, so adding 1 works here as well.\\n105:             int partitionIndex = Math.abs(searchResult + 1);\\n106:             assert partitionIndex < partitionLoadStats.length;\\n107:             partition = partitionLoadStats.partitionIds[partitionIndex];\\n108:         }\\n109: \\n110:         log.trace(\\\"Switching to partition {} in topic {}\\\", partition, topic);\\n111:         return partition;\\n112:     }\\n113: \\n114:     int randomPartition() {\\n115:         return Utils.toPositive(ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextInt());\\n116:     }\\n117: \\n118:     /**\\n119:      * Test-only function.  When partition load stats are defined, return the end of range for the\\n120:      * random number.\\n121:      */\\n122:     public int loadStatsRangeEnd() {\\n123:         assert partitionLoadStats != null;\\n124:         assert partitionLoadStats.length > 0;\\n125:         return partitionLoadStats.cumulativeFrequencyTable[partitionLoadStats.length - 1];\\n126:     }\\n127: \\n128:     /**\\n129:      * Peek currently chosen sticky partition.  This method works in conjunction with {@link #isPartitionChanged}\\n130:      * and {@link #updatePartitionInfo}.  The workflow is the following:\\n131:      *\\n132:      * 1. peekCurrentPartitionInfo is called to know which partition to lock.\\n133:      * 2. Lock partition's batch queue.\\n134:      * 3. isPartitionChanged under lock to make sure that nobody raced us.\\n135:      * 4. Append data to buffer.\\n136:      * 5. updatePartitionInfo to update produced bytes and maybe switch partition.\\n137:      *\\n138:      *  It's important that steps 3-5 are under partition's batch queue lock.\\n139:      *\\n140:      * @param cluster The cluster information (needed if there is no current partition)\\n141:      * @return sticky partition info object\\n142:      */\\n143:     StickyPartitionInfo peekCurrentPartitionInfo(Cluster cluster) {\\n144:         StickyPartitionInfo partitionInfo = stickyPartitionInfo.get();\\n145:         if (partitionInfo != null)\\n146:             return partitionInfo;\\n147: \\n148:         // We're the first to create it.\\n149:         partitionInfo = new StickyPartitionInfo(nextPartition(cluster));\\n150:         if (stickyPartitionInfo.compareAndSet(null, partitionInfo))\\n151:             return partitionInfo;\\n152: \\n153:         // Someone has raced us.\\n154:         return stickyPartitionInfo.get();\\n155:     }\\n156: \\n157:     /**\\n158:      * Check if partition is changed by a concurrent thread.  NOTE this function needs to be called under\\n159:      * the partition's batch queue lock.\\n160:      *\\n161:      * @param partitionInfo The sticky partition info object returned by peekCurrentPartitionInfo\\n162:      * @return true if sticky partition object is changed (race condition)\\n163:      */\\n164:     boolean isPartitionChanged(StickyPartitionInfo partitionInfo) {\\n165:         // partitionInfo may be null if the caller didn't use built-in partitioner.\\n166:         return partitionInfo != null && stickyPartitionInfo.get() != partitionInfo;\\n167:     }\\n168: \\n169:     /**\\n170:      * Update partition info with the number of bytes appended and maybe switch partition.\\n171:      * NOTE this function needs to be called under the partition's batch queue lock.\\n172:      *\\n173:      * @param partitionInfo The sticky partition info object returned by peekCurrentPartitionInfo\\n174:      * @param appendedBytes The number of bytes appended to this partition\\n175:      * @param cluster The cluster information\\n176:      */\\n177:     void updatePartitionInfo(StickyPartitionInfo partitionInfo, int appendedBytes, Cluster cluster) {\\n178:         updatePartitionInfo(partitionInfo, appendedBytes, cluster, true);\\n179:     }\\n180: \\n181:     /**\\n182:      * Update partition info with the number of bytes appended and maybe switch partition.\\n183:      * NOTE this function needs to be called under the partition's batch queue lock.\\n184:      *\\n185:      * @param partitionInfo The sticky partition info object returned by peekCurrentPartitionInfo\\n186:      * @param appendedBytes The number of bytes appended to this partition\\n187:      * @param cluster The cluster information\\n188:      * @param enableSwitch If true, switch partition once produced enough bytes\\n189:      */\\n190:     void updatePartitionInfo(StickyPartitionInfo partitionInfo, int appendedBytes, Cluster cluster, boolean enableSwitch) {\\n191:         // partitionInfo may be null if the caller didn't use built-in partitioner.\\n192:         if (partitionInfo == null)\\n193:             return;\\n194: \\n195:         assert partitionInfo == stickyPartitionInfo.get();\\n196:         int producedBytes = partitionInfo.producedBytes.addAndGet(appendedBytes);\\n197: \\n198:         // We're trying to switch partition once we produce stickyBatchSize bytes to a partition\\n199:         // but doing so may hinder batching because partition switch may happen while batch isn't\\n200:         // ready to send.  This situation is especially likely with high linger.ms setting.\\n201:         // Consider the following example:\\n202:         //   linger.ms=500, producer produces 12KB in 500ms, batch.size=16KB\\n203:         //     - first batch collects 12KB in 500ms, gets sent\\n204:         //     - second batch collects 4KB, then we switch partition, so 4KB gets eventually sent\\n205:         //     - ... and so on - we'd get 12KB and 4KB batches\\n206:         // To get more optimal batching and avoid 4KB fractional batches, the caller may disallow\\n207:         // partition switch if batch is not ready to send, so with the example above we'd avoid\\n208:         // fractional 4KB batches: in that case the scenario would look like this:\\n209:         //     - first batch collects 12KB in 500ms, gets sent\\n210:         //     - second batch collects 4KB, but partition switch doesn't happen because batch in not ready\\n211:         //     - second batch collects 12KB in 500ms, gets sent and now we switch partition.\\n212:         //     - ... and so on - we'd just send 12KB batches\\n213:         // We cap the produced bytes to not exceed 2x of the batch size to avoid pathological cases\\n214:         // (e.g. if we have a mix of keyed and unkeyed messages, key messages may create an\\n215:         // unready batch after the batch that disabled partition switch becomes ready).\\n216:         // As a result, with high latency.ms setting we end up switching partitions after producing\\n217:         // between stickyBatchSize and stickyBatchSize * 2 bytes, to better align with batch boundary.\\n218:         if (producedBytes >= stickyBatchSize * 2) {\\n219:             log.trace(\\\"Produced {} bytes, exceeding twice the batch size of {} bytes, with switching set to {}\\\",\\n220:                 producedBytes, stickyBatchSize, enableSwitch);\\n221:         }\\n222: \\n223:         if (producedBytes >= stickyBatchSize && enableSwitch || producedBytes >= stickyBatchSize * 2) {\\n224:             // We've produced enough to this partition, switch to next.\\n225:             StickyPartitionInfo newPartitionInfo = new StickyPartitionInfo(nextPartition(cluster));\\n226:             stickyPartitionInfo.set(newPartitionInfo);\\n227:         }\\n228:     }\\n229: \\n230:     /**\\n231:      * Update partition load stats from the queue sizes of each partition\\n232:      * NOTE: queueSizes are modified in place to avoid allocations\\n233:      *\\n234:      * @param queueSizes The queue sizes, partitions without leaders are excluded\\n235:      * @param partitionIds The partition ids for the queues, partitions without leaders are excluded\\n236:      * @param length The logical length of the arrays (could be less): we may eliminate some partitions\\n237:      *               based on latency, but to avoid reallocation of the arrays, we just decrement\\n238:      *               logical length\\n239:      * Visible for testing\\n240:      */\\n241:     public void updatePartitionLoadStats(int[] queueSizes, int[] partitionIds, int length) {\\n242:         if (queueSizes == null) {\\n243:             log.trace(\\\"No load stats for topic {}, not using adaptive\\\", topic);\\n244:             partitionLoadStats = null;\\n245:             return;\\n246:         }\\n247:         assert queueSizes.length == partitionIds.length;\\n248:         assert length <= queueSizes.length;\\n249: \\n250:         // The queueSizes.length represents the number of all partitions in the topic and if we have\\n251:         // less than 2 partitions, there is no need to do adaptive logic.\\n252:         // If partitioner.availability.timeout.ms != 0, then partitions that experience high latencies\\n253:         // (greater than partitioner.availability.timeout.ms) may be excluded, the length represents\\n254:         // partitions that are not excluded.  If some partitions were excluded, we'd still want to\\n255:         // go through adaptive logic, even if we have one partition.\\n256:         // See also RecordAccumulator#partitionReady where the queueSizes are built.\\n257:         if (length < 1 || queueSizes.length < 2) {\\n258:             log.trace(\\\"The number of partitions is too small: available={}, all={}, not using adaptive for topic {}\\\",\\n259:                     length, queueSizes.length, topic);\\n260:             partitionLoadStats = null;\\n261:             return;\\n262:         }\\n263: \\n264:         // We build cumulative frequency table from the queue sizes in place.  At the beginning\\n265:         // each entry contains queue size, then we invert it (so it represents the frequency)\\n266:         // and convert to a running sum.  Then a uniformly distributed random variable\\n267:         // in the range [0..last) would map to a partition with weighted probability.\\n268:         // Example: suppose we have 3 partitions with the corresponding queue sizes:\\n269:         //  0 3 1\\n270:         // Then we can invert them by subtracting the queue size from the max queue size + 1 = 4:\\n271:         //  4 1 3\\n272:         // Then we can convert it into a running sum (next value adds previous value):\\n273:         //  4 5 8\\n274:         // Now if we get a random number in the range [0..8) and find the first value that\\n275:         // is strictly greater than the number (e.g. for 4 it would be 5), then the index of\\n276:         // the value is the index of the partition we're looking for.  In this example\\n277:         // random numbers 0, 1, 2, 3 would map to partition[0], 4 would map to partition[1]\\n278:         // and 5, 6, 7 would map to partition[2].\\n279: \\n280:         // Calculate max queue size + 1 and check if all sizes are the same.\\n281:         int maxSizePlus1 = queueSizes[0];\\n282:         boolean allEqual = true;\\n283:         for (int i = 1; i < length; i++) {\\n284:             if (queueSizes[i] != maxSizePlus1)\\n285:                 allEqual = false;\\n286:             if (queueSizes[i] > maxSizePlus1)\\n287:                 maxSizePlus1 = queueSizes[i];\\n288:         }\\n289:         ++maxSizePlus1;\\n290: \\n291:         if (allEqual && length == queueSizes.length) {\\n292:             // No need to have complex probability logic when all queue sizes are the same,\\n293:             // and we didn't exclude partitions that experience high latencies (greater than\\n294:             // partitioner.availability.timeout.ms).\\n295:             log.trace(\\\"All queue lengths are the same, not using adaptive for topic {}\\\", topic);\\n296:             partitionLoadStats = null;\\n297:             return;\\n298:         }\\n299: \\n300:         // Invert and fold the queue size, so that they become separator values in the CFT.\\n301:         queueSizes[0] = maxSizePlus1 - queueSizes[0];\\n302:         for (int i = 1; i < length; i++) {\\n303:             queueSizes[i] = maxSizePlus1 - queueSizes[i] + queueSizes[i - 1];\\n304:         }\\n305:         log.trace(\\\"Partition load stats for topic {}: CFT={}, IDs={}, length={}\\\",\\n306:                 topic, queueSizes, partitionIds, length);\\n307:         partitionLoadStats = new PartitionLoadStats(queueSizes, partitionIds, length);\\n308:     }\\n309: \\n310:     /**\\n311:      * Info for the current sticky partition.\\n312:      */\\n313:     public static class StickyPartitionInfo {\\n314:         private final int index;\\n315:         private final AtomicInteger producedBytes = new AtomicInteger();\\n316: \\n317:         StickyPartitionInfo(int index) {\\n318:             this.index = index;\\n319:         }\\n320: \\n321:         public int partition() {\\n322:             return index;\\n323:         }\\n324:     }\\n325: \\n326:     /*\\n327:      * Default hashing function to choose a partition from the serialized key bytes\\n328:      */\\n329:     public static int partitionForKey(final byte[] serializedKey, final int numPartitions) {\\n330:         return Utils.toPositive(Utils.murmur2(serializedKey)) % numPartitions;\\n331:     }\\n332: \\n333:     /**\\n334:      * The partition load stats for each topic that are used for adaptive partition distribution.\\n335:      */\\n336:     private static final class PartitionLoadStats {\\n337:         public final int[] cumulativeFrequencyTable;\\n338:         public final int[] partitionIds;\\n339:         public final int length;\\n340: \\n341:         public PartitionLoadStats(int[] cumulativeFrequencyTable, int[] partitionIds, int length) {\\n342:             assert cumulativeFrequencyTable.length == partitionIds.length;\\n343:             assert length <= cumulativeFrequencyTable.length;\\n344:             this.cumulativeFrequencyTable = cumulativeFrequencyTable;\\n345:             this.partitionIds = partitionIds;\\n346:             this.length = length;\\n347:         }\\n348:     }\\n349: }\\n350: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01RXPDUTwpQ8faCatVP1V6qb","session_id":"01bdda9f-9010-4ad4-89b9-60148a90d052","uuid":"76a9aa02-a717-40c4-b6ac-57c1ea800405"}

\\n55: /**\\n56:  * A batch of records that is or will be sent.\\n57:  *\\n58:  * This class is not thread safe and external synchronization must be used when modifying it\\n59:  */\\n60: public final this, currentLeaderEpoch, attemptsWhenLeaderLastChanged, latestLeaderEpoch, attempts);\\n123:         }\\n124:     }\\n125: \\n126:     /**\\n127:      * It will return true, for a when batch is being isn't sufficient room.\\n143:      */\\n144:     public FutureRecordMetadata tryAppend(long timestamp, byte[] key, byte[] value, Header[] headers, Callback callback, long now) {\\n145:         if is being used\\n215:      * @return true if the batch was completed as a result of this call, and false\\n216:      *   if it had been completed previously\\n217:      */\\n218:     public boolean The provided top-level exception will be used\\n224:      * for each record future contained in the batch.\\n225:      *\\n226:      * @param topLevelException top-level partition error\\n227:      * @param recordExceptions Record exception function mapping batchIndex to the respective record exception\\n228:      * @return true if the batch was completed as a result of this call, and false\\n229:      *   if it had been completed previously\\n230:      */\\n231:     public boolean completeExceptionally(\\n232:         RuntimeException topLevelException,\\n233:         Function<Integer, RuntimeException> recordExceptions\\n234:     ) {\\n235:         Objects.requireNonNull(topLevelException);\\n236:         Objects.requireNonNull(recordExceptions);\\n237:         return done(ProduceResponse.INVALID_OFFSET, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, topLevelException, recordExceptions);\\n238:     }\\n239: \\n240:     /**\\n241:      * Finalize the state of a batch. Final state, once set, is immutable. This function may be called\\n242:      * once or twice on a batch. It may be called twice if\\n243:      * 1. An inflight batch expires before a response from the broker is received. The batch's final\\n244:      * state is set to FAILED. But it could succeed on the broker and second time around batch.done() may\\n245:      * try to set SUCCEEDED final state.\\n246:      * 2. If a transaction abortion happens or if the producer is closed forcefully, the final state is\\n247:      * ABORTED but again it could succeed if broker responds with a success.\\n248:      *\\n249:      * Attempted transitions from [FAILED | ABORTED] --> SUCCEEDED are logged.\\n250:      * Attempted transitions from one failure state to the same or a different failed state are ignored.\\n251:      * Attempted transitions from SUCCEEDED to the same or a failed state throw an exception.\\n252:      *\\n253:      * @param baseOffset The base offset of the messages assigned by the server\\n254:      * @param logAppendTime The log append time or -1 if CreateTime is being used\\n255:      * @param topLevelException The exception that occurred (or null if the request was successful)\\n256:      * @param recordExceptions Record exception function mapping batchIndex to the respective record exception\\n257:      * @return true if the batch was completed successfully and false if the batch was previously aborted\\n258:      */\\n259:     private boolean done(\\n260:         long baseOffset,\\n261:         long logAppendTime,\\n262:         RuntimeException topLevelException,\\n263:         Function<Integer, RuntimeException> recordExceptions\\n264:     ) {\\n265:         final FinalState tryFinalState = (topLevelException == null) ? FinalState.SUCCEEDED : FinalState.FAILED;\\n266:         if (tryFinalState == FinalState.SUCCEEDED) {\\n267:             log.trace(\\\"Successfully produced messages to {} with base offset {}.\\\", topicPartition, baseOffset);\\n268:         } else {\\n269:             log.trace(\\\"Failed to produce messages to {} with base offset {}.\\\", topicPartition, baseOffset, topLevelException);\\n270:         }\\n271: \\n272:         if (this.finalState.compareAndSet(null, tryFinalState)) {\\n273:             completeFutureAndFireCallbacks(baseOffset, logAppendTime, recordExceptions);\\n274:             return true;\\n275:         }\\n276: \\n277:         if (this.finalState.get() != FinalState.SUCCEEDED) {\\n278:             if (tryFinalState == FinalState.SUCCEEDED) {\\n279:                 // Log if a previously unsuccessful batch succeeded later on.\\n280:                 log.debug(\\\"ProduceResponse returned {} for {} after batch with base offset {} had already been {}.\\\",\\n281:                     tryFinalState, topicPartition, baseOffset, this.finalState.get());\\n282:             } else {\\n283:                 // FAILED --> FAILED and ABORTED --> FAILED transitions are ignored.\\n284:                 log.debug(\\\"Ignored state transition {} -> {} for {} batch with base offset {}\\\",\\n285:                     this.finalState.get(), tryFinalState, topicPartition, baseOffset);\\n286:             }\\n287:         } else {\\n288:             // A SUCCESSFUL batch must not attempt another state change.\\n289:             throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"A \\\" + this.finalState.get() + \\\" batch must not attempt another state change to \\\" + tryFinalState);\\n290:         }\\n291:         return false;\\n292:     }\\n293: \\n294:     private void completeFutureAndFireCallbacks(\\n295:         long baseOffset,\\n296:         long logAppendTime,\\n297:         Function<Integer, RuntimeException> recordExceptions\\n298:     ) {\\n299:         // Set the future before invoking the callbacks as we rely on its state for the `onCompletion` call\\n300:         produceFuture.set(baseOffset, logAppendTime, recordExceptions);\\n301: \\n302:         // execute callbacks\\n303:         for (int i = 0; i < thunks.size(); i++) {\\n304:             try {\\n305:                 Thunk thunk = thunks.get(i);\\n306:                 if (thunk.callback != null) {\\n307:                     if (recordExceptions == null) {\\n308:                         RecordMetadata metadata = thunk.future.value();\\n309:                         thunk.callback.onCompletion(metadata, null);\\n310:                     } else {\\n311:                         RuntimeException exception = recordExceptions.apply(i);\\n312:                         thunk.callback.onCompletion(null, exception);\\n313:                     }\\n314:                 }\\n315:             } catch (Exception e) {\\n316:                 log.error(\\\"Error executing user-provided callback on message for topic-partition '{}'\\\", topicPartition, e);\\n317:             }\\n318:         }\\n319: \\n320:         produceFuture.done();\\n321:     }\\n322: \\n323:     public Deque<ProducerBatch> split(int splitBatchSize) {\\n324:         RecordBatch recordBatch = validateAndGetRecordBatch();\\n325:         Deque<ProducerBatch> batches = splitRecordsIntoBatches(recordBatch, splitBatchSize);\\n326:         finalizeSplitBatches(batches);\\n327:         return batches;\\n328:     }\\n329: \\n330:     private RecordBatch validateAndGetRecordBatch() {\\n331:         MemoryRecords memoryRecords = recordsBuilder.build();\\n332:         Iterator<MutableRecordBatch> recordBatchIter = memoryRecords.batches().iterator();\\n333: \\n334:         if (!recordBatchIter.hasNext())\\n335:             throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Cannot split an empty producer batch.\\\");\\n336: \\n337:         RecordBatch recordBatch = recordBatchIter.next();\\n338:         if (recordBatch.magic() < MAGIC_VALUE_V2 && !recordBatch.isCompressed())\\n339:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Batch splitting cannot be used with non-compressed messages \\\" +\\n340:                     \\\"with version v0 and v1\\\");\\n341: \\n342:         if (recordBatchIter.hasNext())\\n343:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"A producer batch should only have one record batch.\\\");\\n344: \\n345:         return recordBatch;\\n346:     }\\n347: \\n348:     private Deque<ProducerBatch> splitRecordsIntoBatches(RecordBatch recordBatch, int splitBatchSize) {\\n349:         Deque<ProducerBatch> batches = new ArrayDeque<>();\\n350:         Iterator<Thunk> thunkIter = thunks.iterator();\\n351:         // We always allocate batch size because we are already splitting a big batch.\\n352:         // And we also Retain the create time of the original batch.\\n353:         ProducerBatch batch = null;\\n354: \\n355:         for (Record record : recordBatch) {\\n356:             assert thunkIter.hasNext();\\n357:             Thunk thunk = thunkIter.next();\\n358:             if (batch == null)\\n359:                 batch = createBatchOffAccumulatorForRecord(record, splitBatchSize);\\n360: \\n361:             // A newly created batch can always host the first message.\\n362:             if (!batch.tryAppendForSplit(record.timestamp(), record.key(), record.value(), record.headers(), thunk)) {\\n363:                 batches.add(batch);\\n364:                 batch.closeForRecordAppends();\\n365:                 batch = createBatchOffAccumulatorForRecord(record, splitBatchSize);\\n366:                 batch.tryAppendForSplit(record.timestamp(), record.key(), record.value(), record.headers(), thunk);\\n367:             }\\n368:         }\\n369: \\n370:         // Close the last batch and add it to the batch list after split.\\n371:         if (batch != null) {\\n372:             batches.add(batch);\\n373:             batch.closeForRecordAppends();\\n374:         }\\n375: \\n376:         return batches;\\n377:     }\\n378: \\n379:     private void finalizeSplitBatches(Deque<ProducerBatch> batches) {\\n380:         // Chain all split batch ProduceRequestResults to the original batch's produceFuture\\n381:         // Ensures the original batch's future doesn't complete until all split batches complete\\n382:         for (ProducerBatch splitBatch : batches) {\\n383:             produceFuture.addDependent(splitBatch.produceFuture);\\n384:         }\\n385: \\n386:         produceFuture.set(ProduceResponse.INVALID_OFFSET, NO_TIMESTAMP, index -> new RecordBatchTooLargeException());\\n387:         produceFuture.done();\\n388: \\n389:         assignProducerStateToBatches(batches);\\n390:     }\\n391: \\n392:     private void assignProducerStateToBatches(Deque<ProducerBatch> batches) {\\n393:         if (hasSequence()) {\\n394:             int sequence = baseSequence();\\n395:             ProducerIdAndEpoch producerIdAndEpoch = new ProducerIdAndEpoch(producerId(), producerEpoch());\\n396:             for (ProducerBatch newBatch : batches) {\\n397:                 newBatch.setProducerState(producerIdAndEpoch, sequence, isTransactional());\\n398:                 sequence += newBatch.recordCount;\\n399:             }\\n400:         }\\n401:     }\\n402: \\n403:     private ProducerBatch createBatchOffAccumulatorForRecord(Record record, int batchSize) {\\n404:         int initialSize = Math.max(AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytesUpperBound(magic(),\\n405:                 recordsBuilder.compression().type(), record.key(), record.value(), record.headers()), batchSize);\\n406:         ByteBuffer buffer = ByteBuffer.allocate(initialSize);\\n407: \\n408:         // Note that we intentionally do not set producer state (producerId, epoch, sequence, and isTransactional)\\n409:         // for the newly created batch. This will be set when the batch is dequeued for sending (which is consistent\\n410:         // with how normal batches are handled).\\n411:         MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = MemoryRecords.builder(buffer, magic(), recordsBuilder.compression(),\\n412:                 TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, 0L);\\n413:         return new ProducerBatch(topicPartition, builder, this.createdMs, true);\\n414:     }\\n415: \\n416:     public boolean isCompressed() {\\n417:         return recordsBuilder.compression().type() != CompressionType.NONE;\\n418:     }\\n419: \\n420:     /**\\n421:      * A callback and the associated FutureRecordMetadata argument to pass to it.\\n422:      */\\n423:     private static final class Thunk {\\n424:         final Callback callback;\\n425:         final FutureRecordMetadata future;\\n426: \\n427:         Thunk(Callback callback, FutureRecordMetadata future) {\\n428:             this.callback = callback;\\n429:             this.future = future;\\n430:         }\\n431:     }\\n432: \\n433:     @Override\\n434:     public String toString() {\\n435:         return \\\"ProducerBatch(topicPartition=\\\" + topicPartition + \\\", recordCount=\\\" + recordCount + \\\")\\\";\\n436:     }\\n437: \\n438:     boolean hasReachedDeliveryTimeout(long deliveryTimeoutMs, long now) {\\n439:         return deliveryTimeoutMs <= now -
