java.util.Objects;\n70: import java.util.Optional;\n71: import java.util.Set;\n72: import java.util.function.Function;\n73: import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n74: \n75: /**\n76:  * The background thread that handles the sending of produce requests to the Kafka cluster. This thread makes metadata\n77:  * requests to renew its view of the cluster and then sends produce requests to the appropriate nodes.\n78:  */\n79: public class Sender implements Runnable {\n80: \n81:     private final Logger log;\n82: \n83:     /* the state of each nodes connection */\n84:     private final KafkaClient client;\n85: \n86:     /* the record accumulator that batches records */\n87:     private final RecordAccumulator accumulator;\n88: \n89:     /* the metadata for the client */\n90:     private final ProducerMetadata metadata;\n91: \n92:     /* the flag indicating whether the producer should guarantee the message order on the broker or not. */\n93:     private final boolean guaranteeMessageOrder;\n94: \n95:     /* the maximum request size to attempt to send to the server */\n96:     private final int maxRequestSize;\n97: \n98:     /* the number of acknowledgements to request from the server */\n99:     private final short acks;\n100: \n101:     /* the number of times to retry a failed request before giving up */\n102:     private final int retries;\n103: \n104:     /* the clock instance used for getting the time */\n105:     private final Time time;\n106: \n107:     /* true while the sender thread is still running */\n108:     private volatile boolean running;\n109: \n110:     /* true when the caller wants to ignore all unsent/inflight messages and force close.  */\n111:     private volatile boolean forceClose;\n112: \n113:     /* metrics */\n114:     private final SenderMetrics sensors;\n115: \n116:     /* the max time to wait for the server to respond to the request*/\n117:     private final int requestTimeoutMs;\n118: \n119:     /* The max time to wait before retrying a request which has failed */\n120:     private final long retryBackoffMs;\n121: \n122:     /* all the state related to transactions, in particular the producer id, producer epoch, and sequence numbers */\n123:     private final TransactionManager transactionManager;\n124: \n125:     // A per-partition queue of batches ordered by creation time for tracking the in-flight batches\n126:     private final Map<TopicPartition, List<ProducerBatch>> inFlightBatches;\n127: \n128:     public Sender(LogContext logContext,\n129:                   KafkaClient client,\n130:                   ProducerMetadata metadata,\n131:                   RecordAccumulator accumulator,\n132:                   boolean guaranteeMessageOrder,\n133:                   int maxRequestSize,\n134:                   short acks,\n135:                   int retries,\n136:                   SenderMetricsRegistry metricsRegistry,\n137:                   Time time,\n138:                   int requestTimeoutMs,\n139:                   long retryBackoffMs,\n140:                   TransactionManager transactionManager) {\n141:         this.log = logContext.logger(Sender.class);\n142:         this.client = client;\n143:         this.accumulator = accumulator;\n144:         this.metadata = metadata;\n145:         this.guaranteeMessageOrder = guaranteeMessageOrder;\n146:         this.maxRequestSize = maxRequestSize;\n147:         this.running = true;\n148:         this.acks = acks;\n149:         this.retries = retries;\n150:         this.time = time;\n151:         this.sensors = new SenderMetrics(metricsRegistry, metadata, client, time);\n152:         this.requestTimeoutMs = requestTimeoutMs;\n153:         this.retryBackoffMs = retryBackoffMs;\n154:         this.transactionManager = transactionManager;\n155:         this.inFlightBatches = new HashMap<>();\n156:     }\n157: \n158:     public List<ProducerBatch> inFlightBatches(TopicPartition tp) {\n159:         return inFlightBatches.containsKey(tp) ? inFlightBatches.get(tp) : new ArrayList<>();\n160:     }\n161: \n162:     private void maybeRemoveFromInflightBatches(ProducerBatch batch) {\n163:         List<ProducerBatch> batches = inFlightBatches.get(batch.topicPartition);\n164:         if (batches != null) {\n165:             batches.remove(batch);\n166:             if (batches.isEmpty()) {\n167:                 inFlightBatches.remove(batch.topicPartition);\n168:             }\n169:         }\n170:     }\n171: \n172:     private void maybeRemoveAndDeallocateBatch(ProducerBatch batch) {\n173:         maybeRemoveFromInflightBatches(batch);\n174:         this.accumulator.deallocate(batch);\n175:     }\n176: \n177:     /**\n178:      *  Get the in-flight batches that has reached delivery timeout.\n179:      */\n180:     private List<ProducerBatch> getExpiredInflightBatches(long now) {\n181:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches = new ArrayList<>();\n182: \n183:         for (Iterator<Map.Entry<TopicPartition, List<ProducerBatch>>> batchIt = inFlightBatches.entrySet().iterator(); batchIt.hasNext();) {\n184:             Map.Entry<TopicPartition, List<ProducerBatch>> entry = batchIt.next();\n185:             List<ProducerBatch> partitionInFlightBatches = entry.getValue();\n186:             if (partitionInFlightBatches != null) {\n187:                 Iterator<ProducerBatch> iter = partitionInFlightBatches.iterator();\n188:                 while (iter.hasNext()) {\n189:                     ProducerBatch batch = iter.next();\n190:                     if (batch.hasReachedDeliveryTimeout(accumulator.getDeliveryTimeoutMs(), now)) {\n191:                         iter.remove();\n192:                         // expireBatches is called in Sender.sendProducerData, before client.poll.\n193:                         // The !batch.isDone() invariant should always hold. An IllegalStateException\n194:                         // exception will be thrown if the invariant is violated.\n195:                         if (!batch.isDone()) {\n196:                             expiredBatches.add(batch);\n197:                         } else {\n198:                             throw new IllegalStateException(batch.topicPartition + \" batch created at \" +\n199:                                 batch.createdMs + \" gets unexpected final state \" + batch.finalState());\n200:                         }\n201:                     } else {\n202:                         accumulator.maybeUpdateNextBatchExpiryTime(batch);\n203:                         break;\n204:                     }\n205:                 }\n206:                 if (partitionInFlightBatches.isEmpty()) {\n207:                     batchIt.remove();\n208:                 }\n209:             }\n210:         }\n211:         return expiredBatches;\n212:     }\n213: \n214:     private void addToInflightBatches(List<ProducerBatch> batches) {\n215:         for (ProducerBatch batch : batches) {\n216:             List<ProducerBatch> inflightBatchList = inFlightBatches.computeIfAbsent(batch.topicPartition,\n217:                 k -> new ArrayList<>());\n218:             inflightBatchList.add(batch);\n219:         }\n220:     }\n221: \n222:     public void addToInflightBatches(Map<Integer, List<ProducerBatch>> batches) {\n223:         for (List<ProducerBatch> batchList : batches.values()) {\n224:             addToInflightBatches(batchList);\n225:         }\n226:     }\n227: \n228:     private boolean hasPendingTransactionalRequests() {\n229:         return transactionManager != null && transactionManager.hasPendingRequests() && transactionManager.hasOngoingTransaction();\n230:     }\n231: \n232:     /**\n233:      * The main run loop for the sender thread\n234:      */\n235:     @Override\n236:     public void run() {\n237:         log.debug(\"Starting Kafka producer I/O thread.\");\n238: \n239:         // main loop, runs until close is called\n240:         while (running) {\n241:             try {\n242:                 runOnce();\n243:             } catch (Exception e) {\n244:                 log.error(\"Uncaught error in kafka producer I/O thread: \", e);\n245:             }\n246:         }\n247: \n248:         log.debug(\"Beginning shutdown of Kafka producer I/O thread, sending remaining records.\");\n249: \n250:         // okay we stopped accepting requests but there may still be\n251:         // requests in the transaction manager, accumulator or waiting for acknowledgment,\n252:         // wait until these are completed.\n253:         while (!forceClose && ((this.accumulator.hasUndrained() || this.client.inFlightRequestCount() > 0) || hasPendingTransactionalRequests())) {\n254:             try {\n255:                 runOnce();\n256:             } catch (Exception e) {\n257:                 log.error(\"Uncaught error in kafka producer I/O thread: \", e);\n258:             }\n259:         }\n260: \n261:         // Abort the transaction if any commit or abort didn't go through the transaction manager's queue\n262:         while (!forceClose && transactionManager != null && transactionManager.hasOngoingTransaction()) {\n263:             if (!transactionManager.isCompleting()) {\n264:                 log.info(\"Aborting incomplete transaction due to shutdown\");\n265:                 try {\n266:                     // It is possible for the transaction manager to throw errors when aborting. Catch these\n267:                     // so as not to interfere with the rest of the shutdown logic.\n268:                     transactionManager.beginAbort();\n269:                 } catch (Exception e) {\n270:                     log.error(\"Error in kafka producer I/O thread while aborting transaction when during closing: \", e);\n271:                     // Force close in case the transactionManager is in error states.\n272:                     forceClose = true;\n273:                 }\n274:             }\n275:             try {\n276:                 runOnce();\n277:             } catch (Exception e) {\n278:                 log.error(\"Uncaught error in kafka producer I/O thread: \", e);\n279:             }\n280:         }\n281: \n282:         if (forceClose) {\n283:             // We need to fail all the incomplete transactional requests and batches and wake up the threads waiting on\n284:             // the futures.\n285:             if (transactionManager != null) {\n286:                 log.debug(\"Aborting incomplete transactional requests due to forced shutdown\");\n287:                 transactionManager.close();\n288:             }\n289:             log.debug(\"Aborting incomplete batches due to forced shutdown\");\n290:             this.accumulator.abortIncompleteBatches();\n291:         }\n292:         try {\n293:             this.client.close();\n294:         } catch (Exception e) {\n295:             log.error(\"Failed to close network client\", e);\n296:         }\n297: \n298:         log.debug(\"Shutdown of Kafka producer I/O thread has completed.\");\n299:     }\n300: \n301:     /**\n302:      * Run a single iteration of sending\n303:      *\n304:      */\n305:     void runOnce() {\n306:         if (transactionManager != null) {\n307:             try {\n308:                 transactionManager.maybeResolveSequences();\n309: \n310:                 RuntimeException lastError = transactionManager.lastError();\n311: \n312:                 // do not continue sending if the transaction manager is in a failed state\n313:                 if (transactionManager.hasFatalError()) {\n314:                     if (lastError != null)\n315:                         maybeAbortBatches(lastError);\n316:                     client.poll(retryBackoffMs, time.milliseconds());\n317:                     return;\n318:                 }\n319: \n320:                 if (transactionManager.hasAbortableError() && shouldHandleAuthorizationError(lastError)) {\n321:                     return;\n322:                 }\n323: \n324:                 // Check whether we need a new producerId. If so, we will enqueue an InitProducerId\n325:                 // request which will be sent below\n326:                 transactionManager.bumpIdempotentEpochAndResetIdIfNeeded();\n327: \n328:                 if (maybeSendAndPollTransactionalRequest()) {\n329:                     return;\n330:                 }\n331:             } catch (AuthenticationException e) {\n332:                 // This is already logged as error, but propagated here to perform any clean ups.\n333:                 log.trace(\"Authentication exception while processing transactional request\", e);\n334:                 transactionManager.authenticationFailed(e);\n335:             }\n336:         }\n337: \n338:         long currentTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n339:         long pollTimeout = sendProducerData(currentTimeMs);\n340:         client.poll(pollTimeout, currentTimeMs);\n341:     }\n342: \n343:     // We handle {@code TransactionalIdAuthorizationException} and {@code ClusterAuthorizationException} by first\n344:     // failing the inflight requests, then transition the state to UNINITIALIZED so that the user doesn't need to\n345:     // instantiate the producer again.\n346:     private boolean shouldHandleAuthorizationError(RuntimeException exception) {\n347:         if (exception instanceof TransactionalIdAuthorizationException ||\n348:                         exception instanceof ClusterAuthorizationException) {\n349:             transactionManager.failPendingRequests(new AuthenticationException(exception));\n350:             maybeAbortBatches(exception);\n351:             transactionManager.transitionToUninitialized(exception);\n352:             return true;\n353:         }\n354:         return false;\n355:     }\n356: \n357:     private long sendProducerData(long now) {\n358:         MetadataSnapshot metadataSnapshot = metadata.fetchMetadataSnapshot();\n359:         // get the list of partitions with data ready to send\n360:         RecordAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult result = this.accumulator.ready(metadataSnapshot, now);\n361: \n362:         // if there are any partitions whose leaders are not known yet, force metadata update\n363:         if (!result.unknownLeaderTopics.isEmpty()) {\n364:             // The set of topics with unknown leader contains topics with leader election pending as well as\n365:             // topics which may have expired. Add the topic again to metadata to ensure it is included\n366:             // and request metadata update, since there are messages to send to the topic.\n367:             for (String topic : result.unknownLeaderTopics)\n368:                 this.metadata.add(topic, now);\n369: \n370:             log.debug(\"Requesting metadata update due to unknown leader topics from the batched records: {}\",\n371:                 result.unknownLeaderTopics);\n372:             this.metadata.requestUpdate(false);\n373:         }\n374: \n375:         // remove any nodes we aren't ready to send to\n376:         Iterator<Node> iter = result.readyNodes.iterator();\n377:         long notReadyTimeout = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n378:         while (iter.hasNext()) {\n379:             Node node = iter.next();\n380:             if (!this.client.ready(node, now)) {\n381:                 // Update just the readyTimeMs of the latency stats, so that it moves forward\n382:                 // every time the batch is ready (then the difference between readyTimeMs and\n383:                 // drainTimeMs would represent how long data is waiting for the node).\n384:                 this.accumulator.updateNodeLatencyStats(node.id(), now, false);\n385:                 iter.remove();\n386:                 notReadyTimeout = Math.min(notReadyTimeout, this.client.pollDelayMs(node, now));\n387:             } else {\n388:                 // Update both readyTimeMs and drainTimeMs, this would \"reset\" the node\n389:                 // latency.\n390:                 this.accumulator.updateNodeLatencyStats(node.id(), now, true);\n391:             }\n392:         }\n393: \n394:         // create produce requests\n395:         Map<Integer, List<ProducerBatch>> batches = this.accumulator.drain(metadataSnapshot, result.readyNodes, this.maxRequestSize, now);\n396:         addToInflightBatches(batches);\n397:         if (guaranteeMessageOrder) {\n398:             // Mute all the partitions drained\n399:             for (List<ProducerBatch> batchList : batches.values()) {\n400:                 for (ProducerBatch batch : batchList)\n401:                     this.accumulator.mutePartition(batch.topicPartition);\n402:             }\n403:         }\n404: \n405:         accumulator.resetNextBatchExpiryTime();\n406:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredInflightBatches = getExpiredInflightBatches(now);\n407:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches = this.accumulator.expiredBatches(now);\n408:         expiredBatches.addAll(expiredInflightBatches);\n409: \n410:         // Reset the producer id if an expired batch has previously been sent to the broker. Also update the metrics\n411:         // for expired batches. see the documentation of @TransactionState.resetIdempotentProducerId to understand why\n412:         // we need to reset the producer id here.\n413:         if (!expiredBatches.isEmpty())\n414:             log.trace(\"Expired {} batches in accumulator\", expiredBatches.size());\n415:         for (ProducerBatch expiredBatch : expiredBatches) {\n416:             String errorMessage = \"Expiring \" + expiredBatch.recordCount + \" record(s) for \" + expiredBatch.topicPartition\n417:                 + \":\" + (now - expiredBatch.createdMs) + \" ms has passed since batch creation\";\n418:             failBatch(expiredBatch, new TimeoutException(errorMessage), false);\n419:             if (transactionManager != null && expiredBatch.inRetry()) {\n420:                 // This ensures that no new batches are drained until the current in flight batches are fully resolved.\n421:                 transactionManager.markSequenceUnresolved(expiredBatch);\n422:             }\n423:         }\n424:         sensors.updateProduceRequestMetrics(batches);\n425: \n426:         // If we have any nodes that are ready to send + have sendable data, poll with 0 timeout so this can immediately\n427:         // loop and try sending more data. Otherwise, the timeout will be the smaller value between next batch expiry\n428:         // time, and the delay time for checking data availability. Note that the nodes may have data that isn't yet\n429:         // sendable due to lingering, backing off, etc. This specifically does not include nodes with sendable data\n430:         // that aren't ready to send since they would cause busy looping.\n431:         long pollTimeout = Math.min(result.nextReadyCheckDelayMs, notReadyTimeout);\n432:         pollTimeout = Math.min(pollTimeout, this.accumulator.nextExpiryTimeMs() - now);\n433:         pollTimeout = Math.max(pollTimeout, 0);\n434:         if (!result.readyNodes.isEmpty()) {\n435:             log.trace(\"Nodes with data ready to send: {}\", result.readyNodes);\n436:             // if some partitions are already ready to be sent, the select time would be 0;\n437:             // otherwise if some partition already has some data accumulated but not ready yet,\n438:             // the select time will be the time difference between now and its linger expiry time;\n439:             // otherwise the select time will be the time difference between now and the metadata expiry time;\n440:             pollTimeout = 0;\n441:         }\n442:         sendProduceRequests(batches, now);\n443:         return pollTimeout;\n444:     }\n445: \n446:     /**\n447:      * Returns true if a transactional request is sent or polled, or if a FindCoordinator request is enqueued\n448:      */\n449:     private boolean maybeSendAndPollTransactionalRequest() {\n450:         if (transactionManager.hasInFlightRequest()) {\n451:             // as long as there are outstanding transactional requests, we simply wait for them to return\n452:             client.poll(retryBackoffMs, time.milliseconds());\n453:             return true;\n454:         }\n455: \n456:         if (transactionManager.hasAbortableError()) {\n457:             accumulator.abortUndrainedBatches(transactionManager.lastError());\n458:         } else if (transactionManager.isAborting()) {\n459:             accumulator.abortUndrainedBatches(new TransactionAbortedException());\n460:         }\n461: \n462:         TransactionManager.TxnRequestHandler nextRequestHandler = transactionManager.nextRequest(accumulator.hasIncomplete());\n463:         if (nextRequestHandler == null)\n464:             return false;\n465: \n466:         AbstractRequest.Builder<?> requestBuilder = nextRequestHandler.requestBuilder();\n467:         Node targetNode = null;\n468:         try {\n469:             FindCoordinatorRequest.CoordinatorType coordinatorType = nextRequestHandler.coordinatorType();\n470:             targetNode = coordinatorType != null ?\n471:                     transactionManager.coordinator(coordinatorType) :\n472:                     client.leastLoadedNode(time.milliseconds()).node();\n473:             if (targetNode != null) {\n474:                 if (!awaitNodeReady(targetNode, coordinatorType)) {\n475:                     log.trace(\"Target node {} not ready within request timeout, will retry when node is ready.\", targetNode);\n476:                     maybeFindCoordinatorAndRetry(nextRequestHandler);\n477:                     return true;\n478:                 }\n479:             } else if (coordinatorType != null) {\n480:                 log.trace(\"Coordinator not known for {}, will retry {} after finding coordinator.\", coordinatorType, requestBuilder.apiKey());\n481:                 maybeFindCoordinatorAndRetry(nextRequestHandler);\n482:                 return true;\n483:             } else {\n484:                 log.trace(\"No nodes available to send requests, will poll and retry when until a node is ready.\");\n485:                 transactionManager.retry(nextRequestHandler);\n486:                 client.poll(retryBackoffMs, time.milliseconds());\n487:                 return true;\n488:             }\n489: \n490:             if (nextRequestHandler.isRetry())\n491:                 time.sleep(nextRequestHandler.retryBackoffMs());\n492: \n493:             long currentTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n494:             ClientRequest clientRequest = client.newClientRequest(targetNode.idString(), requestBuilder, currentTimeMs,\n495:                 true, requestTimeoutMs, nextRequestHandler);\n496:             log.debug(\"Sending transactional request {} to node {} with correlation ID {}\", requestBuilder, targetNode, clientRequest.correlationId());\n497:             client.send(clientRequest, currentTimeMs);\n498:             transactionManager.setInFlightCorrelationId(clientRequest.correlationId());\n499:             client.poll(retryBackoffMs, time.milliseconds());\n500:             return true;\n501:         } catch (IOException e) {\n502:             log.debug(\"Disconnect from {} while trying to send request {}. Going \" +\n503:                     \"to back off and retry.\", targetNode, requestBuilder, e);\n504:             // We break here so that we pick up the FindCoordinator request immediately.\n505:             maybeFindCoordinatorAndRetry(nextRequestHandler);\n506:             return true;\n507:         }\n508:     }\n509: \n510:     private void maybeFindCoordinatorAndRetry(TransactionManager.TxnRequestHandler nextRequestHandler) {\n511:         if (nextRequestHandler.needsCoordinator()) {\n512:             transactionManager.lookupCoordinator(nextRequestHandler);\n513:         } else {\n514:             // For non-coordinator requests, sleep here to prevent a tight loop when no node is available\n515:             time.sleep(retryBackoffMs);\n516:             metadata.requestUpdate(false);\n517:         }\n518: \n519:         transactionManager.retry(nextRequestHandler);\n520:     }\n521: \n522:     private void maybeAbortBatches(RuntimeException exception) {\n523:         if (accumulator.hasIncomplete()) {\n524:             log.error(\"Aborting producer batches due to fatal error\", exception);\n525:             accumulator.abortBatches(exception);\n526:         }\n527:     }\n528: \n529:     /**\n530:      * Start closing the sender (won't actually complete until all data is sent out)\n531:      */\n532:     public void initiateClose() {\n533:         // Ensure accumulator is closed first to guarantee that no more appends are accepted after\n534:         // breaking from the sender loop. Otherwise, we may miss some callbacks when shutting down.\n535:         this.accumulator.close();\n536:         this.running = false;\n537:         this.wakeup();\n538:     }\n539: \n540:     /**\n541:      * Closes the sender without sending out any pending messages.\n542:      */\n543:     public void forceClose() {\n544:         this.forceClose = true;\n545:         initiateClose();\n546:     }\n547: \n548:     public boolean isRunning() {\n549:         return running;\n550:     }\n551: \n552:     private boolean awaitNodeReady(Node node, FindCoordinatorRequest.CoordinatorType coordinatorType) throws IOException {\n553:         if (NetworkClientUtils.awaitReady(client, node, time, requestTimeoutMs)) {\n554:             if (coordinatorType == FindCoordinatorRequest.CoordinatorType.TRANSACTION) {\n555:                 // Indicate to the transaction manager that the coordinator is ready, allowing it to check ApiVersions\n556:                 // This allows us to bump transactional epochs even if the coordinator is temporarily unavailable at\n557:                 // the time when the abortable error is handled\n558:                 transactionManager.handleCoordinatorReady();\n559:             }\n560:             return true;\n561:         }\n562:         return false;\n563:     }\n564: \n565:     /**\n566:      * Handle a produce response\n567:      */\n568:     private void handleProduceResponse(ClientResponse response, Map<TopicPartition, ProducerBatch> batches, Map<Uuid, String> topicNames, long now) {\n569:         RequestHeader requestHeader = response.requestHeader();\n570:         int correlationId = requestHeader.correlationId();\n571:         if (response.wasTimedOut()) {\n572:             log.trace(\"Cancelled request with header {} due to the last request to node {} timed out\",\n573:                 requestHeader, response.destination());\n574:             for (ProducerBatch batch : batches.values())\n575:                 completeBatch(batch, new ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse(Errors.REQUEST_TIMED_OUT, String.format(\"Disconnected from node %s due to timeout\", response.destination())),\n576:                         correlationId, now, null);\n577:         } else if (response.wasDisconnected()) {\n578:             log.trace(\"Cancelled request with header {} due to node {} being disconnected\",\n579:                 requestHeader, response.destination());\n580:             for (ProducerBatch batch : batches.values())\n581:                 completeBatch(batch, new ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse(Errors.NETWORK_EXCEPTION, String.format(\"Disconnected from node %s\", response.destination())),\n582:                         correlationId, now, null);\n583:         } else if (response.versionMismatch() != null) {\n584:             log.warn(\"Cancelled request {} due to a version mismatch with node {}\",\n585:                     response, response.destination(), response.versionMismatch());\n586:             for (ProducerBatch batch : batches.values())\n587:                 completeBatch(batch, new ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse(Errors.UNSUPPORTED_VERSION), correlationId, now, null);\n588:         } else {\n589:             log.trace(\"Received produce response from node {} with correlation id {}\", response.destination(), correlationId);\n590:             // if we have a response, parse it\n591:             if (response.hasResponse()) {\n592:                 // Sender should exercise PartitionProduceResponse rather than ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse\n593:                 // https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/KAFKA-10696\n594:                 ProduceResponse produceResponse = (ProduceResponse) response.responseBody();\n595:                 // This will be set by completeBatch.\n596:                 Map<TopicPartition, Metadata.LeaderIdAndEpoch> partitionsWithUpdatedLeaderInfo = new HashMap<>();\n597:                 produceResponse.data().responses().forEach(r -> r.partitionResponses().forEach(p -> {\n598:                     ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse partResp = new ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse(\n599:                             Errors.forCode(p.errorCode()),\n600:                             p.baseOffset(),\n601:                             p.logAppendTimeMs(),\n602:                             p.logStartOffset(),\n603:                             p.recordErrors()\n604:                                 .stream()\n605:                                 .map(e -> new ProduceResponse.RecordError(e.batchIndex(), e.batchIndexErrorMessage()))\n606:                                 .collect(Collectors.toList()),\n607:                             p.errorMessage(),\n608:                             p.currentLeader());\n609: \n610:                     // Version 13 drop topic name and add support to topic id.\n611:                     // We need to find batch based on topic id and partition index only as\n612:                     // topic name in the response will be empty.\n613:                     // For older versions, topic id is zero, and we will find the batch based on the topic name.\n614:                     TopicPartition tp = (!r.topicId().equals(Uuid.ZERO_UUID) && topicNames.containsKey(r.topicId())) ?\n615:                             new TopicPartition(topicNames.get(r.topicId()), p.index()) :\n616:                             new TopicPartition(r.name(), p.index());\n617: \n618:                     ProducerBatch batch = batches.get(tp);\n619:                     if (batch == null) {\n620:                         throw new IllegalStateException(\"Can't find batch created for topic id \" + r.topicId() +\n621:                                 \" topic name \" + r.name() + \" partition \" + p.index() + \" using \" + topicNames);\n622:                     }\n623:                     completeBatch(batch, partResp, correlationId, now, partitionsWithUpdatedLeaderInfo);\n624:                 }));\n625: \n626:                 if (!partitionsWithUpdatedLeaderInfo.isEmpty()) {\n627:                     List<Node> leaderNodes = produceResponse.data().nodeEndpoints().stream()\n628:                         .map(e -> new Node(e.nodeId(), e.host(), e.port(), e.rack()))\n629:                         .filter(e -> !e.equals(Node.noNode()))\n630:                         .collect(\n631:                             Collectors.toList());\n632:                     Set<TopicPartition> updatedPartitions = metadata.updatePartitionLeadership(partitionsWithUpdatedLeaderInfo, leaderNodes);\n633:                     if (log.isTraceEnabled()) {\n634:                         updatedPartitions.forEach(\n635:                             part -> log.debug(\"For {} leader was updated.\", part)\n636:                         );\n637:                     }\n638:                 }\n639: \n640:                 this.sensors.recordLatency(response.destination(), response.requestLatencyMs());\n641:             } else {\n642:                 // this is the acks = 0 case, just complete all requests\n643:                 for (ProducerBatch batch : batches.values()) {\n644:                     completeBatch(batch, new ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse(Errors.NONE), correlationId, now, null);\n645:                 }\n646:             }\n647:         }\n648:     }\n649: \n650:     /**\n651:      * Complete or retry the given batch of records.\n652:      *\n653:      * @param batch The record batch\n654:      * @param response The produce response\n655:      * @param correlationId The correlation id for the request\n656:      * @param now The current POSIX timestamp in milliseconds\n657:      * @param partitionsWithUpdatedLeaderInfo This will be populated with partitions that have updated leader info.\n658:      */\n659:     private void completeBatch(ProducerBatch batch, ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse response, long correlationId,\n660:                                long now, Map<TopicPartition, Metadata.LeaderIdAndEpoch> partitionsWithUpdatedLeaderInfo) {\n661:         Errors error = response.error;\n662: \n663:         if (error == Errors.MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE && batch.recordCount > 1 && !batch.isDone() &&\n664:                 (batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 || batch.isCompressed())) {\n665:             // If the batch is too large, we split the batch and send the split batches again. We do not decrement\n666:             // the retry attempts in this case.\n667:             log.warn(\n668:                 \"Got error produce response in correlation id {} on topic-partition {}, splitting and retrying ({} attempts left). Error: {}\",\n669:                 correlationId,\n670:                 batch.topicPartition,\n671:                 this.retries - batch.attempts(),\n672:                 formatErrMsg(response));\n673:             if (transactionManager != null)\n674:                 transactionManager.removeInFlightBatch(batch);\n675:             this.accumulator.splitAndReenqueue(batch);\n676:             maybeRemoveAndDeallocateBatch(batch);\n677:             this.sensors.recordBatchSplit();\n678:         } else if (error != Errors.NONE) {\n679:             if (canRetry(batch, response, now)) {\n680:                 log.warn(\n681:                     \"Got error produce response with correlation id {} on topic-partition {}, retrying ({} attempts left). Error: {}\",\n682:                     correlationId,\n683:                     batch.topicPartition,\n684:                     this.retries - batch.attempts() - 1,\n685:                     formatErrMsg(response));\n686:                 reenqueueBatch(batch, now);\n687:             } else if (error == Errors.DUPLICATE_SEQUENCE_NUMBER) {\n688:                 // If we have received a duplicate sequence error, it means that the sequence number has advanced beyond\n689:                 // the sequence of the current batch, and we haven't retained batch metadata on the broker to return\n690:                 // the correct offset and timestamp.\n691:                 //\n692:                 // The only thing we can do is to return success to the user and not return a valid offset and timestamp.\n693:                 completeBatch(batch, response);\n694:             } else {\n695:                 // tell the user the result of their request. We only adjust sequence numbers if the batch didn't exhaust\n696:                 // its retries -- if it did, we don't know whether the sequence number was accepted or not, and\n697:                 // thus it is not safe to reassign the sequence.\n698:                 failBatch(batch, response, batch.attempts() < this.retries);\n699:             }\n700:             if (error.exception() instanceof InvalidMetadataException) {\n701:                 if (error.exception() instanceof UnknownTopicOrPartitionException) {\n702:                     log.warn(\"Received unknown topic or partition error in produce request on partition {}. The \" +\n703:                             \"topic-partition may not exist or the user may not have Describe access to it\",\n704:                         batch.topicPartition);\n705:                 } else {\n706:                     log.warn(\"Received invalid metadata error in produce request on partition {} due to {} Going \" +\n707:                             \"to request metadata update now\", batch.topicPartition, error.exception(response.errorMessage).toString());\n708:                 }\n709:                 if (error.exception() instanceof NotLeaderOrFollowerException || error.exception() instanceof FencedLeaderEpochException) {\n710:                     log.debug(\"For {}, received error {}, with leaderIdAndEpoch {}\", batch.topicPartition, error, response.currentLeader);\n711:                     if (partitionsWithUpdatedLeaderInfo != null\n712:                         && (response.currentLeader.leaderId() != -1 && response.currentLeader.leaderEpoch() != -1)) {\n713:                         partitionsWithUpdatedLeaderInfo.put(batch.topicPartition, new Metadata.LeaderIdAndEpoch(\n714:                             Optional.of(response.currentLeader.leaderId()), Optional.of(response.currentLeader.leaderEpoch())));\n715:                     }\n716:                 }\n717:                 metadata.requestUpdate(false);\n718:             }\n719:         } else {\n720:             completeBatch(batch, response);\n721:         }\n722: \n723:         // Unmute the completed partition.\n724:         if (guaranteeMessageOrder)\n725:             this.accumulator.unmutePartition(batch.topicPartition);\n726:     }\n727: \n728:     /**\n729:      * Format the error from a {@link ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse} in a user-friendly string\n730:      * e.g \"NETWORK_EXCEPTION. Error Message: Disconnected from node 0\"\n731:      */\n732:     private String formatErrMsg(ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse response) {\n733:         String errorMessageSuffix = (response.errorMessage == null || response.errorMessage.isEmpty()) ?\n734:                 \"\" : String.format(\". Error Message: %s\", response.errorMessage);\n735:         return String.format(\"%s%s\", response.error, errorMessageSuffix);\n736:     }\n737: \n738:     private void reenqueueBatch(ProducerBatch batch, long currentTimeMs) {\n739:         this.accumulator.reenqueue(batch, currentTimeMs);\n740:         maybeRemoveFromInflightBatches(batch);\n741:         this.sensors.recordRetries(batch.topicPartition.topic(), batch.recordCount);\n742:     }\n743: \n744:     private void completeBatch(ProducerBatch batch, ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse response) {\n745:         if (transactionManager != null) {\n746:             transactionManager.handleCompletedBatch(batch, response);\n747:         }\n748: \n749:         if (batch.complete(response.baseOffset, response.logAppendTime)) {\n750:             maybeRemoveAndDeallocateBatch(batch);\n751:         }\n752:     }\n753: \n754:     private void failBatch(ProducerBatch batch,\n755:                            ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse response,\n756:                            boolean adjustSequenceNumbers) {\n757:         final RuntimeException topLevelException;\n758:         if (response.error == Errors.TOPIC_AUTHORIZATION_FAILED)\n759:             topLevelException = new TopicAuthorizationException(Collections.singleton(batch.topicPartition.topic()));\n760:         else if (response.error == Errors.CLUSTER_AUTHORIZATION_FAILED)\n761:             topLevelException = new ClusterAuthorizationException(\"The producer is not authorized to do idempotent sends\");\n762:         else\n763:             topLevelException = response.error.exception(response.errorMessage);\n764: \n765:         if (response.recordErrors == null || response.recordErrors.isEmpty()) {\n766:             failBatch(batch, topLevelException, adjustSequenceNumbers);\n767:         } else {\n768:             Map<Integer, RuntimeException> recordErrorMap = new HashMap<>(response.recordErrors.size());\n769:             for (ProduceResponse.RecordError recordError : response.recordErrors) {\n770:                 // The API leaves us with some awkwardness interpreting the errors in the response.\n771:                 // We cannot differentiate between different error cases (such as INVALID_TIMESTAMP)\n772:                 // from the single error code at the partition level, so instead we use INVALID_RECORD\n773:                 // for all failed records and rely on the message to distinguish the cases.\n774:                 final String errorMessage;\n775:                 if (recordError.message != null) {\n776:                     errorMessage = recordError.message;\n777:                 } else if (response.errorMessage != null) {\n778:                     errorMessage = response.errorMessage;\n779:                 } else {\n780:                     errorMessage = response.error.message();\n781:                 }\n782: \n783:                 // If the batch contained only a single record error, then we can unambiguously\n784:                 // use the exception type corresponding to the partition-level error code.\n785:                 if (response.recordErrors.size() == 1) {\n786:                     recordErrorMap.put(recordError.batchIndex, response.error.exception(errorMessage));\n787:                 } else {\n788:                     recordErrorMap.put(recordError.batchIndex, new InvalidRecordException(errorMessage));\n789:                 }\n790:             }\n791: \n792:             Function<Integer, RuntimeException> recordExceptions = batchIndex -> {\n793:                 RuntimeException exception = recordErrorMap.get(batchIndex);\n794:                 if (exception != null) {\n795:                     return exception;\n796:                 } else {\n797:                     // If the response contains record errors, then the records which failed validation\n798:                     // will be present in the response. To avoid confusion for the remaining records, we\n799:                     // return a generic exception.\n800:                     return new KafkaException(\"Failed to append record because it was part of a batch \" +\n801:                         \"which had one more more invalid records\");\n802:                 }\n803:             };\n804: \n805:             failBatch(batch, topLevelException, recordExceptions, adjustSequenceNumbers);\n806:         }\n807:     }\n808: \n809:     private void failBatch(\n810:         ProducerBatch batch,\n811:         RuntimeException topLevelException,\n812:         boolean adjustSequenceNumbers\n813:     ) {\n814:         failBatch(batch, topLevelException, batchIndex -> topLevelException, adjustSequenceNumbers);\n815:     }\n816: \n817:     private void failBatch(\n818:         ProducerBatch batch,\n819:         RuntimeException topLevelException,\n820:         Function<Integer, RuntimeException> recordExceptions,\n821:         boolean adjustSequenceNumbers\n822:     ) {\n823:         this.sensors.recordErrors(batch.topicPartition.topic(), batch.recordCount);\n824: \n825:         if (batch.completeExceptionally(topLevelException, recordExceptions)) {\n826:             if (transactionManager != null) {\n827:                 try {\n828:                     // This call can throw an exception in the rare case that there's an invalid state transition\n829:                     // attempted. Catch these so as not to interfere with the rest of the logic.\n830:                     transactionManager.handleFailedBatch(batch, topLevelException, adjustSequenceNumbers);\n831:                 } catch (Exception e) {\n832:                     log.debug(\"Encountered error when transaction manager was handling a failed batch\", e);\n833:                 }\n834:             }\n835:             maybeRemoveAndDeallocateBatch(batch);\n836:         }\n837:     }\n838: \n839:     /**\n840:      * We can retry a send if the error is transient and the number of attempts taken is fewer than the maximum allowed.\n841:      * We can also retry OutOfOrderSequence exceptions for future batches, since if the first batch has failed, the\n842:      * future batches are certain to fail with an OutOfOrderSequence exception.\n843:      */\n844:     private boolean canRetry(ProducerBatch batch, ProduceResponse.PartitionResponse response, long now) {\n845:         return !batch.hasReachedDeliveryTimeout(accumulator.getDeliveryTimeoutMs(), now) &&\n846:             batch.attempts() < this.retries &&\n847:             !batch.isDone() &&\n848:             (transactionManager == null ?\n849:                     response.error.exception() instanceof RetriableException :\n850:                     transactionManager.canRetry(response, batch));\n851:     }\n852: \n853:     /**\n854:      * Transfer the record batches into a list of produce requests on a per-node basis\n855:      */\n856:     private void sendProduceRequests(Map<Integer, List<ProducerBatch>> collated, long now) {\n857:         for (Map.Entry<Integer, List<ProducerBatch>> entry : collated.entrySet())\n858:             sendProduceRequest(now, entry.getKey(), acks, requestTimeoutMs, entry.getValue());\n859:     }\n860: \n861:     /**\n862:      * Create a produce request from the given record batches\n863:      */\n864:     private void sendProduceRequest(long now, int destination, short acks, int timeout, List<ProducerBatch> batches) {\n865:         if (batches.isEmpty())\n866:             return;\n867: \n868:         final Map<TopicPartition, ProducerBatch> recordsByPartition = new HashMap<>(batches.size());\n869:         Map<String, Uuid> topicIds = topicIdsForBatches(batches);\n870: \n871:         ProduceRequestData.TopicProduceDataCollection tpd = new ProduceRequestData.TopicProduceDataCollection();\n872:         for (ProducerBatch batch : batches) {\n873:             TopicPartition tp = batch.topicPartition;\n874:             MemoryRecords records = batch.records();\n875:             Uuid topicId = topicIds.get(tp.topic());\n876:             ProduceRequestData.TopicProduceData tpData = tpd.find(tp.topic(), topicId);\n877: \n878:             if (tpData == null) {\n879:                 tpData = new ProduceRequestData.TopicProduceData()\n880:                         .setTopicId(topicId).setName(tp.topic());\n881:                 tpd.add(tpData);\n882:             }\n883: \n884:             tpData.partitionData().add(new ProduceRequestData.PartitionProduceData()\n885:                     .setIndex(tp.partition())\n886:                     .setRecords(records));\n887:             recordsByPartition.put(tp, batch);\n888:         }\n889: \n890:         String transactionalId = null;\n891:         boolean useTransactionV1Version = false;\n892:         if (transactionManager != null && transactionManager.isTransactional()) {\n893:             transactionalId = transactionManager.transactionalId();\n894:             useTransactionV1Version = !transactionManager.isTransactionV2Enabled();\n895:         }\n896: \n897:         ProduceRequest.Builder requestBuilder = ProduceRequest.builder(\n898:                 new ProduceRequestData()\n899:                         .setAcks(acks)\n900:                         .setTimeoutMs(timeout)\n901:                         .setTransactionalId(transactionalId)\n902:                         .setTopicData(tpd),\n903:                 useTransactionV1Version\n904:         );\n905:         // Fetch topic names from metadata outside callback as topic ids may change during the callback\n906:         // for example if topic was recreated.\n907:         Map<Uuid, String> topicNames = metadata.topicNames();\n908: \n909:         RequestCompletionHandler callback = response -> handleProduceResponse(response, recordsByPartition, topicNames, time.milliseconds());\n910: \n911:         String nodeId = Integer.toString(destination);\n912:         ClientRequest clientRequest = client.newClientRequest(nodeId, requestBuilder, now, acks != 0,\n913:                 requestTimeoutMs, callback);\n914:         client.send(clientRequest, now);\n915:         log.trace(\"Sent produce request to {}: {}\", nodeId, requestBuilder);\n916:     }\n917: \n918:     private Map<String, Uuid> topicIdsForBatches(List<ProducerBatch> batches) {\n919:         return batches.stream()\n920:                 .collect(Collectors.toMap(\n921:                         b -> b.topicPartition.topic(),\n922:                         b -> metadata.topicIds().getOrDefault(b.topicPartition.topic(), Uuid.ZERO_UUID),\n923:                         (existing, replacement) -> replacement)\n924:                 );\n925:     }\n926: \n927:     /**\n928:      * Wake up the selector associated with this send thread\n929:      */\n930:     public void wakeup() {\n931:         this.client.wakeup();\n932:     }\n933: \n934:     public static Sensor throttleTimeSensor(SenderMetricsRegistry metrics) {\n935:         Sensor produceThrottleTimeSensor = metrics.sensor(\"produce-throttle-time\");\n936:         produceThrottleTimeSensor.add(metrics.produceThrottleTimeAvg, new Avg());\n937:         produceThrottleTimeSensor.add(metrics.produceThrottleTimeMax, new Max());\n938:         return produceThrottleTimeSensor;\n939:     }\n940: \n941:     /**\n942:      * A collection of sensors for the sender\n943:      */\n944:     private static class SenderMetrics {\n945:         public final Sensor retrySensor;\n946:         public final Sensor errorSensor;\n947:         public final Sensor queueTimeSensor;\n948:         public final Sensor requestTimeSensor;\n949:         public final Sensor recordsPerRequestSensor;\n950:         public final Sensor batchSizeSensor;\n951:         public final Sensor compressionRateSensor;\n952:         public final Sensor maxRecordSizeSensor;\n953:         public final Sensor batchSplitSensor;\n954:         private final SenderMetricsRegistry metrics;\n955:         private final Time time;\n956: \n957:         public SenderMetrics(SenderMetricsRegistry metrics, Metadata metadata, KafkaClient client, Time time) {\n958:             this.metrics = metrics;\n959:             this.time = time;\n960: \n961:             this.batchSizeSensor = metrics.sensor(\"batch-size\");\n962:             this.batchSizeSensor.add(metrics.batchSizeAvg, new Avg());\n963:             this.batchSizeSensor.add(metrics.batchSizeMax, new Max());\n964: \n965:             this.compressionRateSensor = metrics.sensor(\"compression-rate\");\n966:             this.compressionRateSensor.add(metrics.compressionRateAvg, new Avg());\n967: \n968:             this.queueTimeSensor = metrics.sensor(\"queue-time\");\n969:             this.queueTimeSensor.add(metrics.recordQueueTimeAvg, new Avg());\n970:             this.queueTimeSensor.add(metrics.recordQueueTimeMax, new Max());\n971: \n972:             this.requestTimeSensor = metrics.sensor(\"request-time\");\n973:             this.requestTimeSensor.add(metrics.requestLatencyAvg, new Avg());\n974:             this.requestTimeSensor.add(metrics.requestLatencyMax, new Max());\n975: \n976:             this.recordsPerRequestSensor = metrics.sensor(\"records-per-request\");\n977:             this.recordsPerRequestSensor.add(new Meter(metrics.recordSendRate, metrics.recordSendTotal));\n978:             this.recordsPerRequestSensor.add(metrics.recordsPerRequestAvg, new Avg());\n979: \n980:             this.retrySensor = metrics.sensor(\"record-retries\");\n981:             this.retrySensor.add(new Meter(metrics.recordRetryRate, metrics.recordRetryTotal));\n982: \n983:             this.errorSensor = metrics.sensor(\"errors\");\n984:             this.errorSensor.add(new Meter(metrics.recordErrorRate, metrics.recordErrorTotal));\n985: \n986:             this.maxRecordSizeSensor = metrics.sensor(\"record-size\");\n987:             this.maxRecordSizeSensor.add(metrics.recordSizeMax, new Max());\n988:             this.maxRecordSizeSensor.add(metrics.recordSizeAvg, new Avg());\n989: \n990:             this.metrics.addMetric(metrics.requestsInFlight, (config, now) -> client.inFlightRequestCount());\n991:             this.metrics.addMetric(metrics.metadataAge,\n992:                 (config, now) -> (now - metadata.lastSuccessfulUpdate()) / 1000.0);\n993: \n994:             this.batchSplitSensor = metrics.sensor(\"batch-split-rate\");\n995:             this.batchSplitSensor.add(new Meter(metrics.batchSplitRate, metrics.batchSplitTotal));\n996:         }\n997: \n998:         private void maybeRegisterTopicMetrics(String topic) {\n999:             // if one sensor of the metrics has been registered for the topic,\n1000:             // then all other sensors should have been registered; and vice versa\n1001:             String topicRecordsCountName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".records-per-batch\";\n1002:             Sensor topicRecordCount = this.metrics.getSensor(topicRecordsCountName);\n1003:             if (topicRecordCount == null) {\n1004:                 Map<String, String> metricTags = Collections.singletonMap(\"topic\", topic);\n1005: \n1006:                 topicRecordCount = this.metrics.sensor(topicRecordsCountName);\n1007:                 MetricName rateMetricName = this.metrics.topicRecordSendRate(metricTags);\n1008:                 MetricName totalMetricName = this.metrics.topicRecordSendTotal(metricTags);\n1009:                 topicRecordCount.add(new Meter(rateMetricName, totalMetricName));\n1010: \n1011:                 String topicByteRateName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".bytes\";\n1012:                 Sensor topicByteRate = this.metrics.sensor(topicByteRateName);\n1013:                 rateMetricName = this.metrics.topicByteRate(metricTags);\n1014:                 totalMetricName = this.metrics.topicByteTotal(metricTags);\n1015:                 topicByteRate.add(new Meter(rateMetricName, totalMetricName));\n1016: \n1017:                 String topicCompressionRateName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".compression-rate\";\n1018:                 Sensor topicCompressionRate = this.metrics.sensor(topicCompressionRateName);\n1019:                 MetricName m = this.metrics.topicCompressionRate(metricTags);\n1020:                 topicCompressionRate.add(m, new Avg());\n1021: \n1022:                 String topicRetryName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".record-retries\";\n1023:                 Sensor topicRetrySensor = this.metrics.sensor(topicRetryName);\n1024:                 rateMetricName = this.metrics.topicRecordRetryRate(metricTags);\n1025:                 totalMetricName = this.metrics.topicRecordRetryTotal(metricTags);\n1026:                 topicRetrySensor.add(new Meter(rateMetricName, totalMetricName));\n1027: \n1028:                 String topicErrorName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".record-errors\";\n1029:                 Sensor topicErrorSensor = this.metrics.sensor(topicErrorName);\n1030:                 rateMetricName = this.metrics.topicRecordErrorRate(metricTags);\n1031:                 totalMetricName = this.metrics.topicRecordErrorTotal(metricTags);\n1032:                 topicErrorSensor.add(new Meter(rateMetricName, totalMetricName));\n1033:             }\n1034:         }\n1035: \n1036:         public void updateProduceRequestMetrics(Map<Integer, List<ProducerBatch>> batches) {\n1037:             long now = time.milliseconds();\n1038:             for (List<ProducerBatch> nodeBatch : batches.values()) {\n1039:                 int records = 0;\n1040:                 for (ProducerBatch batch : nodeBatch) {\n1041:                     // register all per-topic metrics at once\n1042:                     String topic = batch.topicPartition.topic();\n1043:                     maybeRegisterTopicMetrics(topic);\n1044: \n1045:                     // per-topic record send rate\n1046:                     String topicRecordsCountName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".records-per-batch\";\n1047:                     Sensor topicRecordCount = Objects.requireNonNull(this.metrics.getSensor(topicRecordsCountName));\n1048:                     topicRecordCount.record(batch.recordCount);\n1049: \n1050:                     // per-topic bytes send rate\n1051:                     String topicByteRateName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".bytes\";\n1052:                     Sensor topicByteRate = Objects.requireNonNull(this.metrics.getSensor(topicByteRateName));\n1053:                     topicByteRate.record(batch.estimatedSizeInBytes());\n1054: \n1055:                     // per-topic compression rate\n1056:                     String topicCompressionRateName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".compression-rate\";\n1057:                     Sensor topicCompressionRate = Objects.requireNonNull(this.metrics.getSensor(topicCompressionRateName));\n1058:                     topicCompressionRate.record(batch.compressionRatio());\n1059: \n1060:                     // global metrics\n1061:                     this.batchSizeSensor.record(batch.estimatedSizeInBytes(), now);\n1062:                     this.queueTimeSensor.record(batch.queueTimeMs(), now);\n1063:                     this.compressionRateSensor.record(batch.compressionRatio());\n1064:                     this.maxRecordSizeSensor.record(batch.maxRecordSize, now);\n1065:                     records += batch.recordCount;\n1066:                 }\n1067:                 this.recordsPerRequestSensor.record(records, now);\n1068:             }\n1069:         }\n1070: \n1071:         public void recordRetries(String topic, int count) {\n1072:             long now = time.milliseconds();\n1073:             this.retrySensor.record(count, now);\n1074:             String topicRetryName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".record-retries\";\n1075:             Sensor topicRetrySensor = this.metrics.getSensor(topicRetryName);\n1076:             if (topicRetrySensor != null)\n1077:                 topicRetrySensor.record(count, now);\n1078:         }\n1079: \n1080:         public void recordErrors(String topic, int count) {\n1081:             long now = time.milliseconds();\n1082:             this.errorSensor.record(count, now);\n1083:             String topicErrorName = \"topic.\" + topic + \".record-errors\";\n1084:             Sensor topicErrorSensor = this.metrics.getSensor(topicErrorName);\n1085:             if (topicErrorSensor != null)\n1086:                 topicErrorSensor.record(count, now);\n1087:         }\n1088: \n1089:         public void recordLatency(String node, long latency) {\n1090:             long now = time.milliseconds();\n1091:             this.requestTimeSensor.record(latency, now);\n1092:             if (!node.isEmpty()) {\n1093:                 String nodeTimeName = \"node-\" + node + throughout the Apache Kafka producer subsystem. The rationale is that the class manages batches of records (ProducerBatch objects), not individual records, so the new name better reflects its actual responsibility.\n\n## Files Examined\n\n### Core Source Files\n\n1. **clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java** (FILE RENAME + CLASS RENAME)\n   -
